services.description: Manage Azure Storage accounts and related resources.
services.detailedDescription: Commands in this group interact with the Azure Storage resource provider to create, configure, and inspect storage accounts, their access keys, and SAS tokens.

resourceDescriptions.blobinventorypolicy: Manage blob inventory policies for a storage account.
resourceDescriptions.encryptionscope: Manage encryption scopes for a storage account.
resourceDescriptions.localuser: Manage local users for SFTP on a storage account.
resourceDescriptions.managementpolicy: Manage lifecycle management policies for a storage account.
resourceDescriptions.objectreplicationpolicy: Manage object replication policies between storage accounts.
resourceDescriptions.operation: List available operations for Azure Storage.
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Manage private endpoint connections to a storage account.
resourceDescriptions.privatelinkresource: List private link resources supported by a storage account.
resourceDescriptions.sku: List available storage SKUs in a region.
resourceDescriptions.usage: Show storage resource usage for a subscription.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.account: Create, configure, inspect, and delete Azure Storage accounts in a resource group.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.blobinventorypolicy: Blob inventory policies run on a schedule and generate reports of the blobs in your storage account.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.deletedaccount: View and recover storage accounts that have been soft-deleted and are still within the retention period.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.encryptionscope: Encryption scopes allow you to manage encryption within a storage account with a scope dedicated to a container or an individual blob.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.localuser: Local users are used for SSH File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) access to Azure Blob Storage.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.managementpolicy: Lifecycle management policies automate transitions and deletions of blobs based on conditions you define.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.objectreplicationpolicy: Object replication asynchronously copies block blobs between a source and a destination storage account.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.operation: Enumerates all available resource provider operations for Azure Storage.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Private endpoints allow you to connect to Azure Storage over a private link, eliminating exposure to the public internet.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Enumerates the private link resource types available on a storage account.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.sku: Lists available storage SKUs and their capabilities for a given subscription.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.usage: Retrieves current usage count and limits for storage resources in a given location.

subgroups.description: Manage access keys for a storage account.
subgroups.detailedDescription: List and regenerate the shared access keys that grant full control over a storage account.

subgroups.description: Generate shared access signature (SAS) tokens for a storage account.
subgroups.detailedDescription: Generate account-level or service-level SAS tokens that grant time-limited, scoped access to storage resources.

services.description: Manage Azure Key Vault keys, secrets, and certificates.
services.detailedDescription: Commands in this group interact with Azure Key Vault data-plane APIs to manage cryptographic keys, secrets, certificates, RBAC assignments, backup/restore, and vault settings.

resourceDescriptions.key: Manage cryptographic keys in Azure Key Vault.
resourceDescriptions.secret: Manage secrets stored in Azure Key Vault.
resourceDescriptions.certificate: Manage X.509 certificates in Azure Key Vault.
resourceDescriptions.restore: Restore a vault from a full backup.
resourceDescriptions.roleassignment: Manage Key Vault RBAC role assignments.
resourceDescriptions.roledefinition: Manage Key Vault RBAC role definitions.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.key: Create, import, rotate, back up, and restore RSA and EC keys; invoke cryptographic operations (encrypt, sign, wrap) against Key Vault-hosted key material.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.secret: Set, retrieve, update, and delete secrets; manage versions and lifecycle (backup, restore, soft-delete).
resourceDetailedDescriptions.certificate: Create, import, update, and delete certificates; manage issuance policies, issuers, contacts, operations, and versions.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.backup: Initiate and monitor full backup jobs that write the entire vault contents to an Azure Blob Storage container.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.restore: Initiate selective or full restore jobs from a previously created backup stored in Azure Blob Storage.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.roleassignment: Create, list, and delete role assignments that grant principals access to vault data-plane operations.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.roledefinition: List, retrieve, create, and delete role definitions used by Key Vault data-plane RBAC.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.setting: Read and update account-level settings such as role-based access control and soft-delete behaviour.

subgroups.description: Manage soft-deleted keys.
subgroups.detailedDescription: List, show, purge, and recover keys that have been soft-deleted but not yet permanently removed.

subgroups.description: List all versions of a key.
subgroups.detailedDescription: Retrieve the full version history of a key, including disabled versions.

subgroups.description: Manage the automatic rotation policy for a key.
subgroups.detailedDescription: View and configure the automatic key rotation schedule, including rotation period, expiry time, and notification leads.

subgroups.description: Perform cryptographic operations using a Key Vault key.
subgroups.detailedDescription: Encrypt, decrypt, sign, verify, wrap, unwrap, and release operations backed by a Key Vault key.

subgroups.description: Manage soft-deleted secrets.
subgroups.detailedDescription: List, show, purge, and recover secrets that have been soft-deleted but not yet permanently removed.

subgroups.description: List all versions of a secret.
subgroups.detailedDescription: Retrieve all versions of a secret, including expired and disabled versions.

subgroups.description: Manage soft-deleted certificates.
subgroups.detailedDescription: List, show, purge, and recover certificates that have been soft-deleted but not yet permanently removed.

subgroups.description: List all versions of a certificate.
subgroups.detailedDescription: Retrieve all versions of a certificate, including expired and disabled versions.

subgroups.description: Manage the issuance policy for a certificate.
subgroups.detailedDescription: View and update the policy that controls how a certificate is issued, renewed, and validated.

subgroups.description: Monitor and manage an in-progress certificate operation.
subgroups.detailedDescription: Check status, cancel, or delete a pending certificate creation or renewal operation.

subgroups.description: Manage certificate issuers (CA integrations).
subgroups.detailedDescription: Configure and manage the certificate authorities and CA plug-ins that Key Vault contacts when issuing certificates.

subgroups.description: Manage certificate contact email addresses for lifecycle notifications.
subgroups.detailedDescription: View, set, and delete the list of email addresses that receive certificate lifecycle notifications.

services.description: Manage Azure Managed Identities (user-assigned and system-assigned).
services.detailedDescription: Commands in this group interact with the Microsoft.ManagedIdentity resource provider to create, update, delete, and inspect user-assigned managed identities and their federated identity credentials. System-assigned identities are managed through the resource they are attached to.

resourceDescriptions.federatedidentitycredential: Manage federated identity credentials for a user-assigned managed identity.
resourceDescriptions.operation: List available operations for Azure Managed Identity.
resourceDescriptions.systemassignedidentity: Retrieve the system-assigned identity attached to a resource.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.federatedidentitycredential: Federated identity credentials allow a user-assigned identity to trust tokens from an external identity provider (e.g. GitHub Actions, Kubernetes) without managing secrets.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.operation: Enumerates all available resource provider operations for Azure Managed Identity.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.systemassignedidentity: Retrieves the system-assigned managed identity associated with a given resource scope.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.userassignedidentity: Create, update, delete, and inspect user-assigned managed identities that can be attached to any Azure resource to grant it an identity in Azure AD.

services.description: Manage Azure subscriptions, aliases, and tenant policies.
services.detailedDescription: Commands in this group interact with the Microsoft.Subscription resource provider to cancel, rename, enable, and create subscription aliases. Also includes management of subscription policies for tenant-level governance.

resourceDescriptions.alia: Manage subscription aliases for programmatic subscription creation.
resourceDescriptions.billing-account: Retrieve billing account policies applicable to a subscription.
resourceDescriptions.operation: List available operations for the Subscription resource provider.
resourceDescriptions.policy: Manage subscription policies at the tenant level.
resourceDescriptions.subscription: Manage the lifecycle of Azure subscriptions (cancel, rename, enable, accept ownership).

resourceDetailedDescriptions.alia: Subscription aliases allow creation of additional subscriptions under a billing scope without requiring portal access.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.billing-account: Retrieves the subscription policy configuration set at the billing account level.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.operation: Enumerates all available resource provider operations for Azure Subscription management.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.policy: Tenant-level policies control how subscriptions are created and managed within an Azure AD tenant.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.subscription: Cancel, rename, enable, or accept ownership transfer of an Azure subscription. Requires Owner or contributor role on the subscription.

subgroups.description: Manage subscription policies at the tenant level.
subgroups.detailedDescription: View, create, and update subscription policies that control subscription creation and management within an Azure AD tenant.

services.description: Manage Azure RBAC role assignments, role definitions, and deny assignments.
services.detailedDescription: Commands in this group interact with the Microsoft.Authorization resource provider to manage role-based access control. Covers role assignments (grant/revoke access), role definitions (built-in and custom), deny assignments (explicit deny rules), and provider operations metadata.

resourceDescriptions.roleassignment: Manage role assignments that grant principals access to Azure resources.
resourceDescriptions.roledefinition: Manage role definitions including built-in and custom roles.
resourceDescriptions.permission: List effective permissions on a resource or resource group.
resourceDescriptions.denyassignment: View deny assignments that block specific actions on resources.
resourceDescriptions.provider-operations-metadata: List available operations for Azure resource providers.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.roleassignment: Role assignments associate a security principal (user, group, service principal, or managed identity) with a role definition at a specific scope. Requires Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments/write permission.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.roledefinition: Role definitions specify the allowed actions, not-actions, data actions, and assignable scopes. Use custom role definitions to create least-privilege access tailored to your requirements.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.permission: Lists the effective RBAC permissions for the calling principal on a resource group or specific resource.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.denyassignment: Deny assignments are system-generated restrictions that block specific principals from performing specific actions, even if a role assignment grants access. These are read-only and cannot be created by users.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.provider-operations-metadata: Returns the full list of actions and data actions available for each Azure resource provider, useful for constructing custom role definitions.

subgroups.description: Manage role assignments by full ARM resource ID.
subgroups.detailedDescription: Create, retrieve, and delete role assignments using their full ARM resource identifier rather than scope + name components.

services.description: Manage Azure resource groups, resources, providers, and tags.
services.detailedDescription: Commands in this group interact with the Microsoft.Resources resource provider to manage resource groups, move and update generic resources, register providers, and apply tags. Foundational for any Azure deployment.

resourceDescriptions.operation: List available operations for the Resources resource provider.
resourceDescriptions.provider: Manage Azure resource provider registrations and metadata.
resourceDescriptions.providerresourcetype: List resource types available for a registered provider.
resourceDescriptions.resource: Manage generic Azure resources across resource groups.
resourceDescriptions.tag: Manage tags on Azure resources and resource groups.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.operation: Enumerates all operations available from the Microsoft.Resources resource provider.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.provider: Register, unregister, and inspect Azure resource providers. Provider registration is required before creating resources of a given type.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.providerresourcetype: Lists all resource types exposed by a specific resource provider, including supported API versions and locations.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.resource: Create, update, delete, move, and validate generic Azure resources without targeting a specific resource type. Useful for scripting cross-resource operations.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.resourcegroup: Resource groups are logical containers for Azure resources. Create, update, delete, and export ARM templates for resource groups.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.tag: Apply, update, and remove tags on resources and resource groups to support cost allocation, governance, and resource organization.

subgroups.description: Manage resources by full ARM resource ID.
subgroups.detailedDescription: Create, update, delete, and retrieve Azure resources using their full ARM resource ID rather than path components.

subgroups.description: Move or validate moving resources to a different resource group or subscription.
subgroups.detailedDescription: Move resources to another resource group or subscription. Use validate first to confirm the move is supported.

subgroups.description: Manage resource providers at tenant scope.
subgroups.detailedDescription: Register providers and list available resource types at the management group or tenant level.

subgroups.description: Manage tags at an arbitrary resource scope.
subgroups.detailedDescription: Get, create, update, and delete the full set of tags on any resource by its ARM scope URI.

subgroups.description: Manage predefined tag values for a tag name.
subgroups.detailedDescription: Add or remove specific values from a tag name's predefined list at the subscription level.

services.description: Manage Azure virtual machines, scale sets, availability sets, and compute resources.
services.detailedDescription: Commands in this group interact with the Microsoft.Compute resource provider to create and manage VMs, VMSS, SSH keys, images, dedicated hosts, capacity reservations, and related resources. Requires Contributor or Virtual Machine Contributor roles.

resourceDescriptions.availabilityset: Manage availability sets for VM fault tolerance.
resourceDescriptions.capacityreservation: Manage capacity reservations within a capacity reservation group.
resourceDescriptions.capacityreservationgroup: Manage capacity reservation groups that pre-allocate compute capacity.
resourceDescriptions.dedicatedhost: Manage dedicated physical hosts for single-tenant VM isolation.
resourceDescriptions.dedicatedhostgroup: Manage dedicated host groups that contain dedicated hosts.
resourceDescriptions.image: Manage custom VM images built from generalized VMs or managed disks.
resourceDescriptions.loganalytic: Export compute throttling and request-rate analytics.
resourceDescriptions.operation: List available operations for the Compute resource provider.
resourceDescriptions.proximityplacementgroup: Manage proximity placement groups to co-locate VMs for low latency.
resourceDescriptions.restorepoint: Manage VM restore points for point-in-time recovery.
resourceDescriptions.restorepointcollection: Manage restore point collections that group VM restore points.
resourceDescriptions.sshpublickey: Manage SSH public keys for Linux VM authentication.
resourceDescriptions.usage: Show compute resource usage and quotas for a subscription.
resourceDescriptions.virtual-machine-images-edge-zone: Browse VM images available in edge zones.
resourceDescriptions.virtualmachineextension: Manage extensions installed on a virtual machine.
resourceDescriptions.vmruncommandresource: Manage persisted run command resources on virtual machines.
resourceDescriptions.virtualmachinescalesetextension: Manage extensions on a virtual machine scale set.
resourceDescriptions.virtualmachinescalesetrollingupgrade: Manage rolling upgrades for a scale set.
resourceDescriptions.virtualmachinescalesetvm: Manage individual VM instances within a scale set.
resourceDescriptions.virtualmachinescalesetvmextension: Manage extensions on individual scale set VM instances.
resourceDescriptions.scalesetvmruncommandresource: Manage persisted run command resources on scale set VM instances.
resourceDescriptions.virtualmachinesiz: List available VM sizes in a region.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.availabilityset: Availability sets ensure VMs are distributed across fault and update domains to minimize downtime during planned and unplanned events.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.capacityreservation: Reserve compute capacity within a group to guarantee VM deployments succeed. Billed from reservation creation regardless of VM deployment.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.capacityreservationgroup: A logical grouping of capacity reservations. Create the group first, then add individual size reservations within it.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.dedicatedhost: Dedicated hosts provide single-tenant physical servers within Azure. Useful for compliance requirements and bring-your-own-license scenarios.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.dedicatedhostgroup: A container for dedicated hosts that are located in the same region, availability zone, and fault domain.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.image: Custom images created from generalized VMs or managed disks. Use for deploying consistent VM configurations at scale.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.loganalytic: Export throttled request counts and request rate data to a storage account for analysis.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.operation: Enumerates all available resource provider operations for Azure Compute.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.proximityplacementgroup: Co-locate VMs, VMSS, and availability sets in the same data center segment to minimize network latency.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.restorepoint: Application-consistent or crash-consistent snapshots of a VM's disks and configuration for point-in-time recovery.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.restorepointcollection: A collection of restore points for a VM. Create the collection before creating individual restore points.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.sshpublickey: Store SSH public keys in Azure for reuse when creating Linux VMs. Keys can be referenced by name at VM creation time.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.usage: Returns the current usage counts and maximum quotas for compute resources (cores, VMs, etc.) in a specified location.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtual-machine-images-edge-zone: Browse platform images available in Azure extended zones (edge zone deployments).
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualmachine: Full VM lifecycle: create, start, stop, restart, redeploy, deallocate, delete. Also includes run-command, patch management, and diagnostics.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualmachineextension: VM extensions automate post-deployment configuration (e.g. DSC, custom script, diagnostics agent). Managed per VM.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualmachineextensionimage: Enumerate available extension publishers, types, and versions for a region.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualmachineimage: Browse Azure Marketplace platform images by publisher, offer, SKU, and version.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.vmruncommandresource: Persisted run commands stored as ARM child resources on a VM. Can be re-executed and managed independently of one-time run-command invocations.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualmachinescaleset: Scale sets manage a group of identical load-balanced VMs. Supports autoscale, rolling upgrades, and orchestration modes.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualmachinescalesetextension: Manage extensions applied to all instances in a scale set.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualmachinescalesetrollingupgrade: Coordinate phased OS or extension upgrades across scale set instances to minimize disruption.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualmachinescalesetvm: Manage individual instances within a scale set — start, stop, reimage, or run commands on specific instances.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualmachinescalesetvmextension: Manage extensions on a specific VM instance within a scale set.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.scalesetvmruncommandresource: Persisted run commands stored as ARM child resources on a scale set VM instance.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualmachinesiz: Lists the VM sizes available in a specific location for capacity planning.

services.description: Manage Azure virtual networks, NSGs, load balancers, gateways, and network resources.
services.detailedDescription: Commands in this group interact with the Microsoft.Network resource provider to manage virtual networks, subnets, network security groups, public IP addresses, load balancers, application gateways, firewalls, VPN/ExpressRoute gateways, network watchers, and more.

resourceDescriptions.applicationgateway: Manage Azure Application Gateways for Layer 7 load balancing and WAF.
resourceDescriptions.applicationsecuritygroup: Manage application security groups for micro-segmentation.
resourceDescriptions.azurefirewall: Manage Azure Firewall instances for centralized network security.
resourceDescriptions.bastionhost: Manage Azure Bastion hosts for browser-based VM access.
resourceDescriptions.dscpconfiguration: Manage DSCP configurations for QoS traffic marking.
resourceDescriptions.expressroutecircuit: Manage ExpressRoute circuits for dedicated private connectivity.
resourceDescriptions.firewallpolicy: Manage Azure Firewall policies with rule collections.
resourceDescriptions.loadbalancer: Manage Azure Load Balancers for Layer 4 traffic distribution.
resourceDescriptions.natgateway: Manage NAT Gateways for outbound connectivity.
resourceDescriptions.networksecuritygroup: Manage Network Security Groups (NSGs) for traffic filtering.
resourceDescriptions.networkwatcher: Manage Network Watcher for diagnostics and flow logging.
resourceDescriptions.privateendpoint: Manage private endpoints for private connectivity to Azure services.
resourceDescriptions.privatelinkservice: Manage Private Link services for exposing services privately.
resourceDescriptions.publicipprefix: Manage public IP prefixes for contiguous public IP blocks.
resourceDescriptions.routefilter: Manage route filters for BGP community filtering on ExpressRoute.
resourceDescriptions.routetable: Manage route tables for custom routing.
resourceDescriptions.virtualnetworkgateway: Manage virtual network gateways for VPN and ExpressRoute.
resourceDescriptions.virtualwan: Manage Virtual WAN hubs and connections.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.applicationgateway: Application gateways provide SSL termination, URL routing, WAF, and autoscale for HTTP/HTTPS workloads.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.applicationsecuritygroup: Application security groups allow grouping of VMs and defining network security policies based on those groups.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.azurefirewall: Azure Firewall is a managed, cloud-based network security service protecting Azure Virtual Network resources.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.bastionhost: Azure Bastion provides secure RDP/SSH access to VMs over SSL directly in the Azure portal without public IPs.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.ddoscustompolicy: DDoS custom policies tune protection thresholds for specific public IP addresses.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.ddosprotectionplan: DDoS protection plans provide enhanced protection across all virtual networks associated with the plan.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.dscpconfiguration: DSCP configurations mark network traffic with differentiated services code points for QoS prioritization.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.expressroutecircuit: ExpressRoute circuits provide dedicated private connectivity from on-premises networks to Azure.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.firewallpolicy: Firewall policies centralize rule management across Azure Firewall instances with IDPS, TLS inspection, and URL filtering.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.ipallocation: IP allocation resources manage static IP address blocks for virtual networks.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.loadbalancer: Azure Load Balancer distributes inbound traffic across healthy backend instances at Layer 4.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.natgateway: NAT Gateway provides outbound-only internet connectivity with a static public IP for VMs without public IPs.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.networkinterface: Network interfaces are attachments between VMs and virtual networks providing IP configuration and connectivity.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.networksecuritygroup: NSGs contain security rules that allow or deny inbound/outbound traffic based on source, destination, port, and protocol.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.networkwatcher: Network Watcher provides monitoring, diagnostics, and topology visualization for Azure network resources.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpoint: Private endpoints create a private IP within a VNet for private connectivity to Azure PaaS services.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privatelinkservice: Private Link services allow service providers to expose their services over a private endpoint.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.publicipaddress: Public IP addresses can be assigned to VMs, load balancers, and gateways for internet-facing communication.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.publicipprefix: Public IP prefixes are contiguous ranges of public IP addresses for consistent outbound SNAT with NAT Gateway or load balancers.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.routefilter: Route filters control which BGP community routes are received from Microsoft on ExpressRoute Microsoft peering.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.routetable: Route tables contain custom routes that override Azure's default routing for subnets.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualnetwork: Virtual networks are the fundamental building block for private networking in Azure, with subnets, peering, and service endpoints.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualnetworkgateway: VNet gateways connect virtual networks to on-premises networks over IPsec/IKE VPN or ExpressRoute.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualwan: Virtual WAN provides optimized and automated branch connectivity to Azure through a managed hub-and-spoke architecture.

services.description: Manage Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server instances, databases, and configurations.
services.detailedDescription: Covers the full lifecycle of PostgreSQL Flexible Servers: create, update, delete, start, stop, and restart. Also manages databases, firewall rules, configurations, backups, replicas, migrations, private endpoints, and Microsoft Entra administrators. Requires Contributor or PostgreSQL-specific RBAC roles on the target subscription or resource group.

resourceDescriptions.administrators-microsoft-entra: Manage Microsoft Entra (Azure AD) administrators for a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.advancedthreatprotectionsetting: Manage Advanced Threat Protection settings for a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.backups-automatic-and-on-demand: Manage automatic and on-demand backups for a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.backups-long-term-retention: Manage long-term retention backups for a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.capabilities-by-location: List available capabilities (SKUs, versions) for a given Azure region.
resourceDescriptions.capabilities-by-server: List capabilities available for an existing PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.capturedlog: List captured logs for a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.configuration: Manage server configuration parameters for a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.database: Manage databases within a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.firewallrule: Manage firewall rules for a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.migration: Manage migrations to Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.name-availability: Check name availability for PostgreSQL Flexible Server resources.
resourceDescriptions.operation: List available operations for Azure Database for PostgreSQL.
resourceDescriptions.private-dns-zone-suffix: Get the private DNS zone suffix for PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Manage private endpoint connections for a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.privatelinkresource: List private link resources available for a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.quotausage: List quota usages for Azure Database for PostgreSQL in a region.
resourceDescriptions.replica: List read replicas for a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.server: Manage Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server instances.
resourceDescriptions.serverthreatprotectionsetting: Manage threat protection settings for a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.tuningoption: Manage intelligent tuning options and recommendations for a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.virtual-network-subnet-usage: Check virtual network subnet usage for PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.virtualendpoint: Manage virtual endpoints for PostgreSQL Flexible Server read replicas.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.administrators-microsoft-entra: Add, remove, and list Microsoft Entra (Azure AD) principals that have administrator access to a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.advancedthreatprotectionsetting: Configure Microsoft Defender for Cloud threat detection settings on a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.backups-automatic-and-on-demand: View and trigger on-demand backups; list automatic backups for point-in-time restore scenarios.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.backups-long-term-retention: Configure and manage long-term backup retention policies beyond the standard 7–35 day window.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.capabilities-by-location: Query available server SKUs, storage options, and PostgreSQL versions for a specific Azure location before provisioning.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.capabilities-by-server: Query the capabilities that are applicable to an existing server, including available version upgrades.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.capturedlog: Retrieve server logs captured for diagnostic and auditing purposes.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.configuration: View and update PostgreSQL server parameters such as max_connections, work_mem, and shared_buffers.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.database: Create, list, and delete databases hosted within a PostgreSQL Flexible Server instance.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.firewallrule: Create and manage IP-based firewall rules that allow client access to a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.migration: Orchestrate single-server to flexible-server migrations, including prerequisite checks, cancellation, and status tracking.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.name-availability: Verify whether a proposed server name is available globally or within a specific Azure location.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.operation: Enumerate all REST operations exposed by the Microsoft.DBforPostgreSQL resource provider.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.private-dns-zone-suffix: Retrieve the DNS zone suffix used for private access mode, needed when configuring custom DNS.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Approve, reject, or remove private endpoint connections to a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privatelinkresource: List the group IDs available for private link connections to a PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.quotausage: Check subscription-level quota consumption for PostgreSQL Flexible Server resources in a region.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.replica: List read replicas associated with a primary PostgreSQL Flexible Server for high-availability and read-scale scenarios.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.server: Full lifecycle management of PostgreSQL Flexible Server: provision, configure, start/stop/restart, and delete instances.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.serverthreatprotectionsetting: Enable or update server-level threat protection to detect anomalous database access patterns.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.tuningoption: Enable intelligent performance tuning features and retrieve index/query recommendations generated by the tuning engine.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtual-network-subnet-usage: Check whether a subnet is available and not already delegated before deploying a flexible server into a VNet.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualendpoint: Create and manage virtual endpoints that provide a stable connection string pointing to the current primary or read replica.

services.description: Manage Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server instances, databases, and configurations.
services.detailedDescription: Covers the full lifecycle of MySQL Flexible Servers: create, update, delete, start, stop, restart, and failover. Also manages databases, firewall rules, configurations, backups, replicas, private endpoints, and Azure AD administrators. Requires Contributor or MySQL-specific RBAC roles on the target subscription or resource group.

resourceDescriptions.advancedthreatprotectionsetting: Manage Advanced Threat Protection settings for a MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.azureadadministrator: Manage Azure Active Directory administrators for a MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.backup: Manage backups for a MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.backup-and-export: Perform backup and export operations for a MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.check-name-availability: Check name availability for MySQL Flexible Server resources in a specific location.
resourceDescriptions.check-name-availability-without-location: Check name availability for MySQL Flexible Server resources without specifying a location.
resourceDescriptions.check-virtual-network-subnet-usage: Check virtual network subnet usage for MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.configuration: Manage server configuration parameters for a MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.database: Manage databases within a MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.firewallrule: Manage firewall rules for a MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.get-private-dns-zone-suffix: Get the private DNS zone suffix for MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.location-based-capability-set: List and get capability sets available for MySQL Flexible Server in a specific location.
resourceDescriptions.locationbasedcapability: List capabilities available for MySQL Flexible Server in a specific location.
resourceDescriptions.logfile: List log files for a MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.long-running-backup: Initiate long-running backup operations for a MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.ltr-backup: Manage long-running backups for a MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.maintenance: Manage scheduled maintenance windows for a MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.operation-progress: Get the progress of a long-running operation for MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.operationresult: Get the result of a long-running operation for MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Manage private endpoint connections for a MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.privatelinkresource: List private link resources available for a MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.replica: List read replicas for a MySQL Flexible Server.
resourceDescriptions.server: Manage Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server instances.
resourceDescriptions.servers-migration: Manage migration cutover for MySQL Flexible Server.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.channel: Create and manage channels that connect a partner namespace to subscriber partner topics for event routing.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.client: Register and manage MQTT devices or applications that publish and subscribe to topics in an Event Grid namespace.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.clientgroup: Define groups of MQTT clients for applying permission bindings across multiple devices at once.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.domain: Create and manage Event Grid domains that host multiple topics under a single endpoint.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.domaineventsubscription: Subscribe to events from all topics or specific topics within an Event Grid domain.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.domaintopic: Create and manage individual topics within an Event Grid domain.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.domaintopiceventsubscription: Subscribe to events from a specific topic within an Event Grid domain.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.eventsubscription: Create and manage event subscriptions for routing events to endpoints; supports webhooks, Event Hubs, queues, and more.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.extensiontopic: Get the extension topic associated with an Azure resource for system event subscription.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.namespace: Create and manage Event Grid namespaces that provide MQTT broker and pull-based event delivery capabilities.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.namespacetopic: Create and manage topics within a namespace for pull-based event delivery to consumers.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.namespacetopiceventsubscription: Subscribe consumers to namespace topics for pull-based event delivery.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.operation: Enumerate all operations exposed by the Microsoft.EventGrid resource provider.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.partnerconfiguration: Configure which partner event sources are authorized to publish to your subscription.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.partnernamespace: Create and manage partner namespaces for partners to deliver events to subscribers.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.partnerregistration: Register an event source as an Event Grid partner.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.partnertopic: Create and manage partner topics that expose events from registered partner sources.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.partnertopiceventsubscription: Subscribe to events published by a partner topic.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.permissionbinding: Define fine-grained publish/subscribe permissions for MQTT client groups on topic spaces.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Approve, reject, or remove private endpoint connections for Event Grid namespaces and domains.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Enumerate the private link group IDs available for creating private endpoints.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.systemtopic: Create and manage system topics that surface built-in Azure service events (e.g., Storage blob created).
resourceDetailedDescriptions.systemtopiceventsubscription: Subscribe to events surfaced by an Event Grid system topic.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.topic: Create and manage custom Event Grid topics to which your applications publish events.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.topiceventsubscription: Subscribe to events from a custom Event Grid topic.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.topicspace: Define MQTT topic wildcard patterns that clients in a namespace can publish or subscribe to.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.topictype: Query the catalog of Azure services that publish events to Event Grid.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.verifiedpartner: Browse the list of verified Event Grid partners available for creating partner topics.

services.description: Manage Azure Notification Hubs namespaces and notification hubs.
services.detailedDescription: Covers the full lifecycle of Notification Hubs namespaces and hubs: create, update, delete, list credentials, test sends, and manage authorization rules. Also manages private endpoints. Requires Contributor or Notification Hubs Contributor roles.

resourceDescriptions.notificationhub: Manage notification hubs within a namespace.
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Manage private endpoint connections for a Notification Hubs namespace.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.namespace: Full lifecycle of Notification Hubs namespaces: provision, update, delete, check availability, and manage authorization rules.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.notificationhub: Create and manage notification hubs including credential configuration (APNS, FCM, WNS, etc.) and test sends.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Approve, reject, or remove private endpoint connections to restrict namespace access within a VNet.

services.description: Manage Azure Relay namespaces, hybrid connections, and WCF relays.
services.detailedDescription: Covers the full lifecycle of Relay namespaces: create, update, delete. Also manages hybrid connections and WCF relays with their authorization rules, and private endpoints. Requires Contributor or Azure Relay-specific roles.

resourceDescriptions.hybridconnection: Manage hybrid connections within an Azure Relay namespace.
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Manage private endpoint connections for an Azure Relay namespace.
resourceDescriptions.privatelinkresource: List private link resources available for an Azure Relay namespace.
resourceDescriptions.wcfrelay: Manage WCF relays within an Azure Relay namespace.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.hybridconnection: Create and manage hybrid connections that enable on-premises services to be securely exposed through the Azure Relay service.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.namespace: Full lifecycle of Relay namespaces: provision, update, delete, check name availability, and manage authorization rules.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Approve, reject, or remove private endpoint connections to restrict namespace access within a VNet.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Enumerate private link group IDs available for creating private endpoints to a Relay namespace.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.wcfrelay: Create and manage WCF relays for legacy SOAP-based applications that communicate through Azure Relay.

services.description: Manage Azure SignalR Service instances, replicas, and private endpoints.
services.detailedDescription: Covers the full lifecycle of SignalR Service instances: create, update, delete, list keys, regenerate keys. Also manages replicas, custom domains, custom certificates, shared private link resources, and private endpoints. Requires Contributor or SignalR Contributor roles.

resourceDescriptions.customcertificate: Manage custom TLS certificates for an Azure SignalR Service instance.
resourceDescriptions.customdomain: Manage custom domain names for an Azure SignalR Service instance.
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Manage private endpoint connections for an Azure SignalR Service instance.
resourceDescriptions.privatelinkresource: List private link resources available for an Azure SignalR Service instance.
resourceDescriptions.replica: Manage replicas of an Azure SignalR Service instance for geo-redundancy.
resourceDescriptions.replicasharedprivatelinkresource: Manage shared private link resources for SignalR Service replicas.
resourceDescriptions.sharedprivatelinkresource: Manage shared private link resources for an Azure SignalR Service instance.
resourceDescriptions.usage: List usage quotas for Azure SignalR Service in a region.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.customcertificate: Upload and manage custom TLS certificates used with custom domain names on a SignalR Service instance.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.customdomain: Bind custom domain names to a SignalR Service instance for client connections.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Approve, reject, or remove private endpoint connections to restrict SignalR Service access within a VNet.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Enumerate private link group IDs available for creating private endpoints.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.replica: Add geo-redundancy by creating replicas of a SignalR Service instance in other Azure regions.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.replicasharedprivatelinkresource: Configure shared private links on SignalR Service replicas for outbound connectivity to dependent services.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.sharedprivatelinkresource: Configure outbound private connections from SignalR Service to backend services like Azure Functions or App Service.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.signal-r: Full lifecycle of SignalR Service: provision, update, delete, manage keys, and check name availability.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.usage: Query subscription-level quota and usage for SignalR Service resources in a specific region.

services.description: Manage Azure Web PubSub Service instances, hubs, and private endpoints.
services.detailedDescription: Covers the full lifecycle of Web PubSub Service instances: create, update, delete, list keys, regenerate keys. Also manages hubs, replicas, custom domains, custom certificates, shared private link resources, and private endpoints. Requires Contributor or Web PubSub Contributor roles.

resourceDescriptions.customcertificate: Manage custom TLS certificates for a Web PubSub Service instance.
resourceDescriptions.customdomain: Manage custom domain names for a Web PubSub Service instance.
resourceDescriptions.hub: Manage hubs within a Web PubSub Service instance.
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Manage private endpoint connections for a Web PubSub Service instance.
resourceDescriptions.privatelinkresource: List private link resources available for a Web PubSub Service instance.
resourceDescriptions.replica: Manage replicas of a Web PubSub Service instance for geo-redundancy.
resourceDescriptions.replicasharedprivatelinkresource: Manage shared private link resources for Web PubSub Service replicas.
resourceDescriptions.sharedprivatelinkresource: Manage shared private link resources for a Web PubSub Service instance.
resourceDescriptions.usage: List usage quotas for Azure Web PubSub Service in a region.
resourceDescriptions.web-pub-sub: Manage Azure Web PubSub Service instances.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.customcertificate: Upload and manage custom TLS certificates for use with custom domain names.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.customdomain: Bind custom domain names to a Web PubSub Service instance.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.hub: Configure hub settings including event handlers and permissions for client connections.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Approve, reject, or remove private endpoint connections to restrict service access within a VNet.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Enumerate private link group IDs available for creating private endpoints.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.replica: Add geo-redundancy by creating replicas in other Azure regions.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.replicasharedprivatelinkresource: Configure shared private links on replicas for outbound connectivity.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.sharedprivatelinkresource: Configure outbound private connections from Web PubSub Service to backend services.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.usage: Query subscription-level quota and usage for Web PubSub resources in a specific region.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.web-pub-sub: Full lifecycle of Web PubSub Service: provision, update, delete, manage keys, and check name availability.

services.description: Manage Azure DNS zones, record sets, and resource reference lookups.
services.detailedDescription: Covers DNS zone lifecycle (create, update, delete) and management of all record set types (A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, NS, PTR, SOA, SRV, TXT, CAA). Also supports resolving which DNS zone owns a given Azure resource. Requires Contributor or DNS Zone Contributor roles.

resourceDescriptions.recordset: Manage DNS record sets within an Azure DNS zone.
resourceDescriptions.resource-reference: Look up DNS zones that reference a given Azure resource.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.recordset: Create and manage DNS record sets of any type (A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, NS, PTR, SOA, SRV, TXT, CAA) within a zone.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.resource-reference: Resolve which DNS zones contain records pointing to a specific Azure resource ID.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.zone: Full lifecycle of Azure DNS zones: create, update, delete, list records, and export zone data.

services.description: Manage Azure Private DNS zones, record sets, and VNet links.
services.detailedDescription: Covers Private DNS zone lifecycle (create, update, delete) and management of all record set types for private name resolution. Also manages virtual network links to associate VNets with private zones. Requires Contributor or Private DNS Zone Contributor roles.

resourceDescriptions.recordset: Manage DNS record sets within an Azure Private DNS zone.
resourceDescriptions.virtualnetworklink: Manage virtual network links to associate VNets with a Private DNS zone.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.privatezone: Full lifecycle of Private DNS zones used for private name resolution within Azure virtual networks.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.recordset: Create and manage DNS record sets (A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, PTR, SOA, SRV, TXT) within a private DNS zone.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualnetworklink: Link virtual networks to a private DNS zone to enable automatic DNS registration and private name resolution.

services.description: Manage Azure DNS Private Resolver instances, endpoints, and forwarding rule sets.
services.detailedDescription: Covers the full lifecycle of DNS Private Resolvers: create, update, delete. Also manages inbound/outbound endpoints, DNS forwarding rule sets, forwarding rules, virtual network links, DNS security rules, domain lists, and policies. Requires Contributor or DNS Resolver Contributor roles.

resourceDescriptions.dnsforwardingruleset: Manage DNS forwarding rule sets for conditional DNS forwarding.
resourceDescriptions.dnssecurityrule: Manage DNS security rules for blocking or allowing DNS queries.
resourceDescriptions.domainlist: Manage domain lists used in DNS security policies.
resourceDescriptions.forwardingrule: Manage forwarding rules within a DNS forwarding rule set.
resourceDescriptions.inboundendpoint: Manage inbound endpoints for a DNS Private Resolver.
resourceDescriptions.outboundendpoint: Manage outbound endpoints for a DNS Private Resolver.
resourceDescriptions.policy: Manage DNS security policies for a virtual network.
resourceDescriptions.policyvirtualnetworklink: Manage virtual network links for DNS security policies.
resourceDescriptions.resolver: Manage Azure DNS Private Resolver instances.
resourceDescriptions.virtualnetworklink: Manage virtual network links for DNS forwarding rule sets.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.dnsforwardingruleset: Create and manage sets of DNS forwarding rules that conditionally forward queries for specific domains to target DNS servers.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.dnssecurityrule: Define allow/block/alert rules based on domain lists to filter DNS queries traversing the resolver.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.domainlist: Maintain lists of domain names used as match criteria in DNS security rules.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.forwardingrule: Create conditional forwarding rules that redirect queries for a domain to specified target IP addresses.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.inboundendpoint: Provision inbound endpoints that receive DNS queries from on-premises or other networks via ExpressRoute/VPN.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.outboundendpoint: Provision outbound endpoints used to forward DNS queries from Azure VNets to on-premises or external resolvers.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.policy: Apply DNS security policies to control query filtering for resources within a virtual network.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.policyvirtualnetworklink: Link virtual networks to a DNS security policy to apply filtering rules.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.resolver: Full lifecycle of DNS Private Resolver instances: create, update, delete, and manage associations.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualnetworklink: Associate virtual networks with a forwarding rule set so the VNet can use its forwarding rules.

services.description: Manage Azure Traffic Manager profiles, endpoints, and traffic analytics.
services.detailedDescription: Covers the full lifecycle of Traffic Manager profiles and endpoints for global traffic routing (priority, weighted, performance, geographic, subnet, multivalue). Also provides heat map data and user metrics key management. Requires Contributor or Traffic Manager Contributor roles.

resourceDescriptions.endpoint: Manage endpoints within a Traffic Manager profile.
resourceDescriptions.geographichierarchy: Get the geographic hierarchy used for geographic traffic routing.
resourceDescriptions.heat-map: Get Traffic Manager heat map data showing traffic patterns.
resourceDescriptions.usermetricskey: Manage user metrics keys for client-side Traffic Manager metrics.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.endpoint: Create and manage Traffic Manager endpoints (Azure, external, nested) with weights, priorities, and geo-location settings.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.geographichierarchy: Retrieve the complete geographic hierarchy used when configuring geographic routing method endpoints.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.heat-map: Retrieve traffic heat map data showing source IP locations and latency distributions for a Traffic Manager profile.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.profile: Full lifecycle of Traffic Manager profiles: create, update, delete, check DNS availability, and list nested endpoints.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.usermetricskey: Generate and manage keys for client-side JavaScript performance metrics collection.

services.description: Manage Azure CDN profiles, endpoints, origins, and Azure Front Door (Standard/Premium) resources
services.detailedDescription: Covers the full lifecycle of classic CDN profiles and endpoints (origins, origin groups, custom domains) and Azure Front Door Standard/Premium resources (AFD profiles, endpoints, origin groups, routes, rule sets, secrets, security policies). Includes WAF policies, log analytics, and resource usage queries. Requires Contributor or CDN-specific roles on the target subscription or resource group.

resourceDescriptions.profile: A CDN or Azure Front Door profile grouping endpoints and configuration.
resourceDescriptions.endpoint: A CDN endpoint under a classic CDN profile, representing a distinct URL hostname.
resourceDescriptions.origin: An origin server backing a CDN endpoint.
resourceDescriptions.origingroup: A group of origins for load balancing under a CDN endpoint.
resourceDescriptions.customdomain: A custom domain mapped to a CDN endpoint.
resourceDescriptions.afdprofile: Azure Front Door profile-level utilities: resource usage, secret validation, and endpoint name availability.
resourceDescriptions.afdendpoint: An Azure Front Door endpoint under an AFD profile.
resourceDescriptions.afdorigingroup: A group of origins for load balancing under an AFD endpoint.
resourceDescriptions.afdorigin: An origin server backing an AFD origin group.
resourceDescriptions.afdcustomdomain: A custom domain mapped to an AFD endpoint.
resourceDescriptions.route: A routing rule mapping URL patterns to origin groups in AFD.
resourceDescriptions.ruleset: A named set of delivery rules applied to AFD requests.
resourceDescriptions.rule: An individual delivery rule within an AFD rule set.
resourceDescriptions.secret: A secret (certificate or token) stored in an AFD profile.
resourceDescriptions.securitypolicy: A WAF security policy linked to an AFD endpoint.
resourceDescriptions.policy: A WAF policy for classic CDN profiles.
resourceDescriptions.loganalytic: Log analytics queries for CDN and AFD traffic metrics and rankings.
resourceDescriptions.managedruleset: Managed WAF rule sets available for CDN WAF policies.
resourceDescriptions.resource-usage: Subscription-level CDN resource quota and usage.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.profile: Profiles are the top-level container for CDN or AFD configuration. Classic CDN profiles use Endpoint sub-resources; AFD profiles use AFDEndpoint, AFDOriginGroup, and related sub-resources. Use `can-migrate` and `migrate` to convert a classic CDN profile to AFD.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.endpoint: Endpoints define the delivery configuration for a CDN hostname. Each endpoint can have multiple origins, origin groups, and custom domains. Use `start`/`stop` to control traffic, and `purge-content`/`load-content` to manage the cache.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.origin: Origins are the upstream servers that CDN fetches content from. An origin belongs to exactly one endpoint (classic CDN). Set priority and weight for load balancing across origins.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.origingroup: Origin groups enable health probing and load balancing across multiple origins within a classic CDN endpoint.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.customdomain: Custom domains map a user-owned hostname to a CDN endpoint. Use `enable-https` / `disable-https` to manage TLS.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.afdprofile: AFD profile-level operations: check endpoint name availability, check hostname availability, list resource usage, validate secrets, and upgrade from Standard to Premium.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.afdendpoint: AFD endpoints are the entry points for Front Door traffic. Each endpoint maps to a hostname and routes traffic via routes to origin groups. Use `purge-content` to invalidate cached content.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.afdorigingroup: AFD origin groups define health probe settings and load balancing across origins. Each AFD endpoint's routes point to an origin group.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.afdorigin: AFD origins are the upstream servers within an AFD origin group. Configure priority, weight, and host header overrides per origin.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.afdcustomdomain: AFD custom domains associate user-owned hostnames with AFD endpoints. Use `refresh-validation-token` to renew the domain ownership validation.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.route: Routes map URL patterns (path, protocol) to an AFD origin group, enabling traffic steering and caching rules.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.ruleset: Rule sets group delivery rules that can be applied to one or more routes.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.rule: Individual delivery rules within a rule set. Each rule has match conditions and actions (redirect, rewrite, cache override, etc.).
resourceDetailedDescriptions.secret: Secrets store customer-managed TLS certificates (from Key Vault) or URL signing keys for use in AFD custom domains and security policies.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.securitypolicy: Security policies associate a WAF policy with one or more AFD endpoints, enforcing bot protection and custom rules.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.policy: WAF policies for classic CDN profiles. Define managed rule sets and custom rules to inspect and block malicious traffic.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.loganalytic: Query CDN and AFD traffic analytics: top URLs, referrers, user agents, countries, and WAF-specific metrics and rankings.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.edgenode: Lists the Azure CDN point-of-presence (PoP) locations used to serve cached content globally.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.managedruleset: Lists the Microsoft-managed WAF rule sets (e.g. DefaultRuleSet, BotRuleSet) available for use in CDN WAF policies.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.resource-usage: Queries CDN resource quota and current usage at the subscription level (profiles, endpoints, etc.).

services.description: Manage Azure Front Door (classic) profiles, routing rules, WAF policies, and network experiment profiles
services.detailedDescription: Covers the full lifecycle of classic Azure Front Door resources: front doors, frontend endpoints, routing rules, backend pools, rules engines, WAF policies, and Network Experiment (Latency Scorecard/Timeseries) resources. For new deployments, prefer Azure Front Door Standard/Premium (CDN-based) via the `cdn` command group. Requires Contributor or Front Door-specific roles on the target subscription or resource group.

resourceDescriptions.frontdoor: A classic Azure Front Door profile that provides global load balancing, SSL offload, and WAF.
resourceDescriptions.frontendendpoint: A frontend endpoint (custom domain or default azurefd.net hostname) within a Front Door.
resourceDescriptions.rulesengine: A rules engine configuration for advanced traffic routing within a Front Door.
resourceDescriptions.policy: A WAF policy (Web Application Firewall) for classic Front Door.
resourceDescriptions.networkexperimentprofile: A Network Experiment profile for measuring latency and routing scorecards.
resourceDescriptions.experiment: A network experiment measuring performance between two endpoints.
resourceDescriptions.preconfiguredendpoint: A pre-configured Microsoft endpoint available for use in network experiments.
resourceDescriptions.report: Latency scorecard and timeseries reports for network experiments.
resourceDescriptions.managedruleset: Managed WAF rule sets available for Front Door WAF policies.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.frontdoor: Classic Front Door profiles contain frontend endpoints, backend pools, routing rules, health probe settings, and load balancing settings. Use `validate-custom-domain` to check custom domain mapping. For new projects, prefer the Azure Front Door Standard/Premium tier.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.frontendendpoint: Frontend endpoints define the hostnames that Front Door accepts traffic on. Use `enable-https` / `disable-https` to manage TLS certificates for custom domains.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.rulesengine: Rules engines apply conditional logic to route requests based on headers, URLs, and other attributes, overriding global routing rules.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.policy: WAF policies attach managed rule sets and custom rules to Front Door to block malicious traffic. Use `list` to see all policies in a resource group or `list-by-subscription` for subscription-wide view.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.networkexperimentprofile: Network Experiment profiles group experiments for latency measurement between Azure and external endpoints.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.experiment: Experiments measure real-user latency between two endpoints (e.g., Azure region vs. on-premises). Results appear in reports.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.preconfiguredendpoint: Pre-configured Microsoft endpoints (Azure regions, CDN PoPs) for use as experiment targets.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.report: Use `get-latency-scorecards` and `get-timeseries` to retrieve experiment measurement data for analysis.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.managedruleset: Lists the Microsoft-managed WAF rule sets (e.g., DefaultRuleSet, BotRuleSet) available for classic Front Door WAF policies.

services.description: Manage Azure App Service web apps, app service plans, environments, certificates, and static sites
services.detailedDescription: Covers the full lifecycle of Azure App Service resources: web apps and deployment slots, app service plans, app service environments (ASE v2/v3), managed certificates, Kubernetes environments (Arc), static web apps, and Logic Apps Standard workflows embedded in App Service. Requires Contributor or Website Contributor roles on the target subscription or resource group.

resourceDescriptions.s: An Azure App Service web app, function app, or Logic App Standard instance.
resourceDescriptions.appserviceplan: An App Service plan defining the compute tier and scaling settings for hosted apps.
resourceDescriptions.appserviceenvironment: An App Service Environment (ASE) providing a dedicated, network-isolated hosting infrastructure.
resourceDescriptions.certificate: An App Service managed certificate for custom domain TLS.
resourceDescriptions.staticsite: An Azure Static Web Apps site with built-in CI/CD and serverless API support.
resourceDescriptions.workflow: A Logic App Standard workflow embedded in an App Service web app.
resourceDescriptions.workflowrun: A run instance of a Logic App Standard workflow.
resourceDescriptions.workflowrunaction: An action execution within a Logic App Standard workflow run.
resourceDescriptions.workflowrunactionrepetition: A repetition of a loop or parallel action within a workflow run.
resourceDescriptions.workflowtrigger: A trigger definition in a Logic App Standard workflow.
resourceDescriptions.workflowtriggerhistory: The trigger history (past executions) for a Logic App Standard workflow trigger.
resourceDescriptions.workflowversion: A versioned snapshot of a Logic App Standard workflow definition.
resourceDescriptions.sitecertificate: A private certificate (PFX) uploaded to an App Service web app.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.s: App Service web apps support multiple runtime stacks (Node.js, Python, .NET, Java, PHP) and feature deployment slots for staging/production swaps. Key operations include `start`, `stop`, `restart`, configuration management, backup/restore, and network integration via VNet and hybrid connections.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.appserviceplan: App Service plans set the pricing tier (Free, Shared, Basic, Standard, Premium, Isolated) and region. Multiple apps share the same plan's compute resources. Use `create-or-update` to provision and `update` to scale.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.appserviceenvironment: ASEs provide single-tenant isolation with direct VNet integration, internal load balancing, and dedicated infrastructure. ASE v3 is the current generation. Supports worker pool management and custom DNS suffix configuration.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.certificate: App Service certificates can be managed (auto-renewed) or imported. They are bound to custom domains on web apps. Use `create-or-update` to provision a free managed certificate.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.kubeenvironment: Kubernetes environments connect Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes clusters to App Service, enabling web app hosting on customer-managed infrastructure.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.staticsite: Static Web Apps provides global hosting with CDN, authentication, API routes, preview environments per PR, and custom domains. Use `create-or-update-static-site` to provision.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflow: Logic Apps Standard workflows embedded in an App Service web app provide stateful and stateless workflow execution with a rich connector ecosystem.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflowrun: Lists and retrieves individual run instances of a Logic App Standard workflow, including input/output and status.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflowrunaction: Details about each action (step) executed within a workflow run, including expression traces for debugging.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflowrunactionrepetition: Lists repetition details for For-Each and Until loop actions within a workflow run.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflowtrigger: Manages trigger definitions in a Logic App Standard workflow: list, run, get callback URL, and schema.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflowtriggerhistory: Views past trigger execution history for a Logic App Standard workflow.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflowversion: Lists and retrieves versioned snapshots of a Logic App Standard workflow definition.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.sitecertificate: Private certificates (PFX) uploaded to a web app for client certificate authentication or Key Vault-based certificate binding.

services.description: Manage Azure Logic Apps workflows, integration accounts, and integration service environments
services.detailedDescription: Covers the full lifecycle of standalone Logic Apps: workflow CRUD, trigger management, run history, and integration account resources (agreements, certificates, maps, partners, schemas, sessions). Also includes Integration Service Environments (ISE) for network-isolated Logic Apps. For Logic Apps Standard (hosted in App Service), use the `webapp` command group instead.

resourceDescriptions.workflow: A Logic Apps workflow defining automation and integration logic.
resourceDescriptions.workflowrun: A run instance of a Logic Apps workflow.
resourceDescriptions.workflowrunaction: An action (step) execution within a workflow run.
resourceDescriptions.workflowrunactionrepetition: A repetition of a loop or parallel action within a workflow run.
resourceDescriptions.workflowrunactionrepetitionsrequesthisto: HTTP request/response history for a workflow run action repetition.
resourceDescriptions.workflowrunactionrequesthistory: HTTP request/response history for a workflow run action.
resourceDescriptions.workflowrunoperation: A long-running operation associated with a workflow run.
resourceDescriptions.workflowrunactionscoperepetition: Scope repetition details for a workflow run action.
resourceDescriptions.workflowtrigger: A trigger definition in a Logic Apps workflow.
resourceDescriptions.workflowtriggerhistory: Past execution history for a workflow trigger.
resourceDescriptions.workflowversion: A versioned snapshot of a Logic Apps workflow definition.
resourceDescriptions.workflowversiontrigger: Trigger callback URL for a specific workflow version.
resourceDescriptions.integrationaccount: An integration account for storing B2B artifacts (schemas, maps, partners, agreements).
resourceDescriptions.integrationaccountagreement: A B2B trading partner agreement (AS2, X12, EDIFACT) in an integration account.
resourceDescriptions.integrationaccountassembly: A .NET assembly registered in an integration account for use in workflows.
resourceDescriptions.integrationaccountbatchconfiguration: A batch configuration for aggregating messages in an integration account.
resourceDescriptions.integrationaccountcertificate: An X.509 certificate stored in an integration account.
resourceDescriptions.integrationaccountmap: An XSLT or Liquid map for message transformation in an integration account.
resourceDescriptions.integrationaccountpartner: A trading partner definition in an integration account.
resourceDescriptions.integrationaccountschema: An XML or JSON schema for message validation in an integration account.
resourceDescriptions.integrationaccountsession: A session for tracking stateful B2B message exchange in an integration account.
resourceDescriptions.integrationserviceenvironment: An Integration Service Environment (ISE) providing network-isolated Logic Apps execution.
resourceDescriptions.integrationserviceenvironmentmanagedapi: A managed connector API available within an ISE.
resourceDescriptions.integrationserviceenvironmentmanagedapio: Operations available on a managed connector API within an ISE.
resourceDescriptions.integrationserviceenvironmentsku: SKU options available for an ISE.
resourceDescriptions.integration-service-environment-network-: Network health status of an Integration Service Environment.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflow: Workflows define the automation logic using a JSON-based definition language. Use `list-by-subscription` and `list-by-resource-group` for discovery, `disable`/`enable` for activation control, and `regenerate-access-key` for securing HTTP triggers.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflowrun: Each workflow execution is captured as a run. Use `list` to view recent runs, `get` for details, and `cancel` to abort in-progress runs.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflowrunaction: Individual actions (HTTP, Switch, Condition, etc.) within a run. Use `list-expression-traces` to debug expression evaluation.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflowrunactionrepetition: For-Each and Until loop iterations are tracked as repetitions. Query these for loop debugging.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflowrunactionrepetitionsrequesthisto: HTTP request/response pairs for action repetitions, useful for auditing outbound calls.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflowrunactionrequesthistory: HTTP request/response pairs for non-repeated actions.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflowrunoperation: Retrieves the status of long-running workflow run operations.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflowrunactionscoperepetition: Scope action repetitions (Scope, Parallel, ForEach scopes).
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflowtrigger: Triggers initiate workflow runs (HTTP, Recurrence, Service Bus, etc.). Use `run` to manually fire a trigger and `list-callback-url` to get the webhook URL.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflowtriggerhistory: Lists past trigger firing events for auditing and replay.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflowversion: Workflow versions capture the definition at each modification. Use `get` to retrieve a historical definition.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflowversiontrigger: Gets the callback URL for a trigger on a specific workflow version.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integrationaccount: Integration accounts store B2B artifacts and are linked to workflows. Use `list-callback-url` and `log-tracking-events` for monitoring.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integrationaccountagreement: Agreements define the terms for B2B message exchange between trading partners.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integrationaccountassembly: Custom .NET assemblies for use in map transforms and expressions.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integrationaccountbatchconfiguration: Batch configurations collect and release messages at scheduled intervals or count thresholds.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integrationaccountcertificate: Certificates used for AS2 message signing/encryption or partner authentication.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integrationaccountmap: XSLT 1.0/2.0 or Liquid maps for transforming XML or JSON message content.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integrationaccountpartner: Partners represent trading companies with their B2B identifiers (DUNS, GLN, etc.).
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integrationaccountschema: XML or JSON schemas used for message validation in workflows.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integrationaccountsession: Sessions enable stateful B2B message sequencing and correlation.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integrationserviceenvironment: ISEs run in a customer VNet for network isolation and dedicated capacity. Use `restart` to recover from issues.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integrationserviceenvironmentmanagedapi: Pre-built connectors (Azure Service Bus, SQL, etc.) deployed within the ISE.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integrationserviceenvironmentmanagedapio: Lists available operations on ISE managed connector APIs.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integrationserviceenvironmentsku: Lists available ISE SKUs (Developer, Premium) and their capacity options.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integration-service-environment-network-: Reports the health of network access from the ISE to dependent services.

services.description: Manage Azure Key Vault and Managed HSM instances, access policies, keys, and secrets
services.detailedDescription: Covers the control-plane lifecycle of Key Vault vaults and Managed HSM clusters: creation, deletion, soft-delete/purge, access policy management, private endpoint connections, and RM-managed keys and secrets. For data-plane operations (encrypt, sign, certificate management), use the `keyvault` command group instead.

resourceDescriptions.vault: An Azure Key Vault instance storing secrets, keys, and certificates.
resourceDescriptions.key: An RM-managed cryptographic key stored in a Key Vault.
resourceDescriptions.secret: An RM-managed secret stored in a Key Vault.
resourceDescriptions.managedhsm: An Azure Managed HSM cluster providing FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated key management.
resourceDescriptions.managedhsmkey: An RM-managed cryptographic key in a Managed HSM.
resourceDescriptions.mhsmprivateendpointconnection: A private endpoint connection to a Managed HSM.
resourceDescriptions.mhsmprivatelinkresource: Private link resources available for a Managed HSM.
resourceDescriptions.mhsmregion: Replication regions configured for a Managed HSM.
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: A private endpoint connection to a Key Vault.
resourceDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Private link resources available for a Key Vault.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.vault: Key Vaults provide centralized secret, key, and certificate storage with RBAC and access policies. Use `update-access-policy` to add, remove, or replace access policy entries. Soft-delete is enabled by default; use `purge-deleted` to permanently remove a deleted vault.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.key: RM-managed keys enable key lifecycle management (create, rotate) via Azure Resource Manager without direct Key Vault data-plane access. Use `list-versions` and `get-version` to inspect key rotation history.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.secret: RM-managed secrets can be created and read via ARM. For full secret management (expiry, content type), use the data-plane `keyvault` command group.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.managedhsm: Managed HSMs provide dedicated single-tenant HSM capacity for high-security key operations. Supports backup/restore for disaster recovery and multi-region replication.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.managedhsmkey: Keys in a Managed HSM. Like RM vault keys, these can be created and versioned via ARM.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.mhsmprivateendpointconnection: Manage private endpoint connections to restrict Managed HSM access to a VNet.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.mhsmprivatelinkresource: Lists the private link resources (sub-resources) available on a Managed HSM for private endpoint targeting.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.mhsmregion: Lists and manages the secondary regions for Managed HSM geo-replication.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Manage private endpoint connections to restrict Key Vault network access.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Lists the private link resources available on a Key Vault.

services.description: Manage Azure Trusted Signing (Code Signing) accounts and certificate profiles
services.detailedDescription: Covers the lifecycle of Azure Trusted Signing accounts and certificate profiles.
