Certificate profiles define the signing certificate type (PublicTrust, PrivateTrust, etc.) and can be used to sign binaries and artifacts. Use `revoke-certificate` to invalidate a previously issued signing certificate.

resourceDescriptions.account: An Azure Trusted Signing account managing code-signing certificates.
resourceDescriptions.certificateprofile: A certificate profile defining the type and properties of signing certificates issued under a Trusted Signing account.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.account: Trusted Signing accounts are the top-level resource. Use `check-name-availability` before creating. Requires Trusted Signing Certificate Profile Signer role to sign.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.certificateprofile: Certificate profiles specify the signing scope (PublicTrust, PrivateTrust, VBSEnclave) and subject name details. Use `revoke-certificate` to invalidate a certificate that was misused or compromised.

services.description: Manage Azure Dedicated HSM and Cloud HSM Cluster resources
services.detailedDescription: Covers both Azure Dedicated HSM (single-tenant physical HSM devices) and Cloud HSM Clusters (multi-node managed HSM services). Dedicated HSMs are for compliance-requiring workloads that need direct PKCS#11 access. Cloud HSM Clusters provide managed HSM capacity with backup/restore and private endpoint support.

resourceDescriptions.cloudhsmcluster: A Cloud HSM Cluster providing managed, multi-node HSM capacity.
resourceDescriptions.cloudhsmclusterbackupstatu: The status of an ongoing Cloud HSM Cluster backup operation.
resourceDescriptions.cloudhsmclusterprivateendpointconnection: A private endpoint connection to a Cloud HSM Cluster.
resourceDescriptions.cloudhsmclusterprivatelinkresource: Private link resources available for a Cloud HSM Cluster.
resourceDescriptions.cloudhsmclusterrestorestatu: The status of an ongoing Cloud HSM Cluster restore operation.
resourceDescriptions.dedicated-hsm: An Azure Dedicated HSM device providing direct PKCS#11 access.
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: A private endpoint connection resource.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.cloudhsmcluster: Cloud HSM Clusters manage a pool of HSM nodes. Use `backup` and `restore` for disaster recovery. Use `validate-backup-properties` and `validate-restore-properties` to pre-validate before executing.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.cloudhsmclusterbackupstatu: Poll this resource after initiating a `backup` operation to track completion.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.cloudhsmclusterprivateendpointconnection: Approve, reject, or delete private endpoint connections to restrict Cloud HSM Cluster network access.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.cloudhsmclusterprivatelinkresource: Lists sub-resources available for private endpoint targeting on a Cloud HSM Cluster.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.cloudhsmclusterrestorestatu: Poll this resource after initiating a `restore` operation to track completion.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.dedicated-hsm: Dedicated HSMs are single-tenant physical devices in Azure data centers. Use `list-outbound-network-dependencies-endpoints` to understand egress requirements.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Private endpoint connections for Cloud HSM Clusters.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.operation: Lists all available operations for the HardwareSecurityModules provider.

services.description: Manage Azure Attestation providers for trusted hardware attestation
services.detailedDescription: Covers the lifecycle of Azure Attestation providers which validate trusted execution environments (TEEs) such as SGX enclaves, AMD SEV-SNP, and TPMs. Use `list-default` to discover Microsoft-managed shared providers, or create a custom provider for customizable attestation policies.

resourceDescriptions.provider: An Azure Attestation provider validating trusted execution environments.
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: A private endpoint connection to an attestation provider.
resourceDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Private link resources available for an attestation provider.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.provider: Attestation providers issue JWT tokens asserting that code is running in a genuine TEE. Use `list-default` to find shared Microsoft-managed providers, or `create` to deploy a custom provider with your own policy.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Manage private endpoint connections to restrict attestation provider access to a VNet.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Lists sub-resources available for private endpoint targeting.

services.description: Manage Azure Confidential Ledger instances for tamper-proof append-only record keeping
services.detailedDescription: Covers the lifecycle of Azure Confidential Ledger instances, which provide blockchain-backed, tamper-proof append-only storage for audit logs and compliance records. Entries are protected by Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs). Use `files-export` to export ledger data.

resourceDescriptions.ledger: An Azure Confidential Ledger instance providing tamper-proof append-only storage.
resourceDescriptions.check-name-availability: Check if a ledger name is available in a region.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.ledger: Confidential Ledgers use SGX-backed TEEs and a distributed ledger to ensure immutability. Data is verified with cryptographic receipts. Use `files-export` to extract ledger entries for offline analysis.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.check-name-availability: Validates that a proposed ledger name is available before provisioning.

services.description: Manage Azure Monitor scheduled query rules, data collection, autoscale, action groups, and alerts
services.detailedDescription: Covers key Azure Monitor resource management: scheduled query alert rules (log-based alerts), data collection rules and endpoints (agent-based data ingestion), autoscale settings for VM scale sets and App Service plans, action groups for alert notifications, and activity log alerts. For metric queries and log queries, use Azure CLI or SDK directly.

resourceDescriptions.scheduledqueryrule: A scheduled query alert rule evaluating KQL or Metric queries on a schedule.
resourceDescriptions.datacollectionrule: A data collection rule (DCR) defining what data to collect and where to send it.
resourceDescriptions.datacollectionendpoint: A data collection endpoint (DCE) providing the ingestion URL for DCR-based data flows.
resourceDescriptions.datacollectionruleassociation: An association linking a DCR to a monitored resource.
resourceDescriptions.autoscalesetting: An autoscale setting defining scale-out/in rules for VM scale sets or App Service plans.
resourceDescriptions.predictive-metric: Predicted metric values used for predictive autoscale in VM scale sets.
resourceDescriptions.actiongroup: An action group defining notification channels (email, SMS, webhook, ITSM) for alerts.
resourceDescriptions.activitylogalert: An activity log alert firing on Azure control-plane events.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.scheduledqueryrule: Scheduled query rules run KQL queries against Log Analytics workspaces or metric queries on a configurable schedule, firing alerts when conditions are met. Supports multi-resource queries and dynamic thresholds.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.datacollectionrule: Data collection rules configure the Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) and the Log Ingestion API to collect performance counters, event logs, syslog, and custom logs, routing them to Log Analytics workspaces or Azure Monitor metrics.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.datacollectionendpoint: Data collection endpoints provide stable HTTPS ingestion URLs for custom log pipelines. Associate DCRs with DCEs to route data through private links.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.datacollectionruleassociation: Associations link DCRs to Azure VMs, VM scale sets, or Arc-enabled servers, applying the data collection configuration to those resources.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.autoscalesetting: Autoscale settings define scale-in/out rules based on metrics (CPU, memory, queue depth), schedules, or predictive capacity models. Apply to VM scale sets, App Service plans, and other scalable resources.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.predictive-metric: Use `get` to retrieve predicted metric values for a VM scale set to inform predictive autoscale planning.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.actiongroup: Action groups are reusable notification configurations attached to alert rules. Supports email, SMS, Azure Functions, Logic Apps, ITSM connectors, and runbooks.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.activitylogalert: Activity log alerts fire on Azure Resource Manager events (resource creation, policy assignments, service health events). Scoped to subscription, resource group, or resource.

services.description: Manage Azure Log Analytics workspaces, clusters, tables, and query packs
services.detailedDescription: Covers the full lifecycle of Log Analytics workspaces (creation, deletion, key management, purge), dedicated clusters, workspace tables (schema, retention, search jobs), saved searches, data exports to Storage/Event Hub, linked services, and query packs for reusable KQL queries. Requires Log Analytics Contributor or Reader roles.

resourceDescriptions.workspace: A Log Analytics workspace storing and querying log and metric data.
resourceDescriptions.deletedworkspace: A soft-deleted Log Analytics workspace pending permanent deletion.
resourceDescriptions.cluster: A Log Analytics dedicated cluster providing capacity commitment pricing and customer-managed keys.
resourceDescriptions.table: A Log Analytics workspace table with configurable schema, retention, and access mode.
resourceDescriptions.savedsearch: A saved KQL query in a Log Analytics workspace.
resourceDescriptions.dataexport: A continuous data export rule routing workspace data to a Storage Account or Event Hub.
resourceDescriptions.linkedservice: A linked service connecting a Log Analytics workspace to another Azure resource (e.g., Automation account).
resourceDescriptions.querypack: A collection of reusable KQL query definitions that can be shared across workspaces.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.workspace: Log Analytics workspaces are the central data store for Azure Monitor logs. Configure retention, daily cap, networking (private link), and customer-managed keys via workspace settings. Use `delete` with 14-day soft-delete for recovery.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.deletedworkspace: Lists soft-deleted workspaces that can be recovered by re-creating with the same name and resource group within 14 days.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.cluster: Dedicated clusters are for organizations ingesting 500+ GB/day needing capacity commitments, CMK, or cross-workspace querying. Link workspaces to clusters via `linkedservice`.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.table: Tables define the schema, retention period, and access tier (Analytics, Basic, Auxiliary) for log data. Use `create-or-update` to configure custom retention or define search jobs.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.savedsearch: Saved searches store frequently-used KQL queries in a workspace for reuse across dashboards and alert rules.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.dataexport: Data export continuously sends workspace table data to a Storage Account or Event Hub for long-term archival or external processing.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.linkedservice: Links external services like Azure Automation to a workspace, enabling runbook-based alerting and other integrations.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.querypack: Query packs store a set of KQL queries as reusable artifacts. Use `create-or-update` and the log analytics portal to manage shared query libraries.

services.description: Manage Azure Application Insights components and availability web tests
services.detailedDescription: Covers the lifecycle of Application Insights components (workspace-based and classic), availability web tests, and data purge operations. Application Insights provides distributed tracing, exception tracking, performance monitoring, and custom telemetry for applications. Requires Application Insights Component Contributor role or equivalent.

resourceDescriptions.component: An Application Insights component providing telemetry collection and analysis for an application.
resourceDescriptions.webtest: An availability web test that periodically pings a URL to check uptime and response time.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.component: Application Insights components can be workspace-based (linked to a Log Analytics workspace) or classic. Use `create-or-update` with the WorkspaceResourceId property to create workspace-based components. Use `purge` to permanently delete telemetry data for GDPR compliance.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.webtest: Availability tests send HTTP requests to a URL at configurable intervals and locations. Failures trigger alerts. Use `list-by-component` to find all tests for a specific Application Insights resource.

services.description: Manage Azure Advisor recommendations, scores, and suppression rules
services.detailedDescription: Provides access to Azure Advisor recommendations for cost, reliability, security, operational excellence, and performance. Use `recommendation generate` to trigger a fresh analysis, `suppression create` to snooze unwanted recommendations, and `score list` to see the Advisor score per category.

resourceDescriptions.recommendation: An Azure Advisor recommendation suggesting an action to improve cost, reliability, security, or performance.
resourceDescriptions.recommendation-metadata: Metadata describing the types and categories of available Advisor recommendations.
resourceDescriptions.configuration: Advisor configuration settings at the subscription or resource group level (e.g., low CPU threshold for downsizing).
resourceDescriptions.score: The Advisor score summarizing your adherence to best practices across recommendation categories.
resourceDescriptions.suppression: A suppression rule that dismisses or snoozes a specific Advisor recommendation.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.recommendation: Recommendations include actionable guidance with impact assessments. Use `generate` to force a fresh scan before listing. Each recommendation includes the affected resource and suggested fix.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.recommendation-metadata: Lists all recommendation types across all categories, useful for building dashboards or automation.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.configuration: Configurations allow tuning Advisor thresholds (e.g., set CPU downsizing threshold to 5% instead of default). Scoped per subscription or resource group.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.score: The Advisor score (0-100) measures adoption of best practices. Use `list` for all categories or `get` for a specific category score.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.suppression: Suppressions let you dismiss recommendations for a period or permanently. Use `delete` to re-activate a suppressed recommendation.

services.description: Manage Azure Monitor Prometheus rule groups for AKS and Arc-enabled clusters
services.detailedDescription: Covers the lifecycle of Prometheus rule groups, which define recording rules and alerting rules for Azure Monitor managed Prometheus in AKS and Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes clusters. Requires Monitoring Contributor role on the target resource group.

resourceDescriptions.prometheusrulegroup: A Prometheus rule group defining recording rules and alerting rules for Azure Monitor managed Prometheus.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.prometheusrulegroup: Prometheus rule groups are evaluated against metrics collected by Azure Monitor managed Prometheus. Use `create-or-update` to define recording rules (pre-aggregated metrics) and alerting rules (conditions that fire Azure Monitor alerts). Scoped to an AKS cluster or Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes cluster.

services.description: Manage Azure Data Factory pipelines, datasets, linked services, triggers, and integration runtimes
services.detailedDescription: Covers the full Data Factory resource lifecycle: factories, pipelines, datasets, linked services, data flows, triggers, integration runtimes, managed virtual networks, and private endpoints. Requires Contributor or Data Factory Contributor role.

resourceDescriptions.dataset: Data factory datasets representing data structures
resourceDescriptions.linkedservice: Connections to external data stores and compute services
resourceDescriptions.trigger: Pipeline execution triggers (schedule, tumbling window, event-based)
resourceDescriptions.integrationruntime: Integration runtimes for data movement and transformation
resourceDescriptions.managedvirtualnetwork: Managed virtual networks for secure data access
resourceDescriptions.managedprivateendpoint: Private endpoints within managed virtual networks
resourceDescriptions.activityrun: Activity run details within pipeline runs
resourceDescriptions.globalparameter: Global parameters shared across the data factory
resourceDescriptions.data-flow-debug-session: Interactive debug sessions for data flows
resourceDescriptions.exposure-control: Feature exposure control for preview features
resourceDescriptions.integration-runtime: Interactive query settings for integration runtimes
resourceDescriptions.integration-runtime-object-metadata: SSIS object metadata for integration runtimes
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Private endpoint connections
resourceDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Available private link resources

resourceDetailedDescriptions.factory: Top-level Azure Data Factory resource. Use create-or-update to provision, configure Git repo integration, and manage access.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.pipeline: Orchestrates data movement and transformation activities. Use create-run to execute a pipeline on demand.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.dataset: Defines the schema and location of data used by activities. References a linked service for connectivity.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.linkedservice: Encapsulates connection information for external sources and sinks (Azure Storage, SQL, Cosmos DB, etc.).
resourceDetailedDescriptions.dataflow: Wrangling or mapping data flow definitions used in data flow activities.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.trigger: Automates pipeline execution on a schedule, tumbling window, or storage/custom events.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integrationruntime: Provides compute infrastructure for activities — Azure IR (cloud), self-hosted IR, or SSIS IR.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integrationruntimenode: Individual machine nodes of a self-hosted integration runtime cluster.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.managedvirtualnetwork: An Azure-managed VNet that isolates the Data Factory integration runtime from the public internet.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.managedprivateendpoint: Private endpoints provisioned inside a managed virtual network.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.pipelinerun: Immutable records of individual pipeline executions with status, duration, and error details.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.activityrun: Detailed results of each activity within a pipeline run.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.triggerrun: Records of trigger firings that initiated pipeline runs.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.globalparameter: Named parameters accessible by all pipelines within the factory.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.credentialoperation: User-assigned managed identity credentials used by linked services.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.change-data-capture: CDC configurations for streaming incremental changes from source systems.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.data-flow-debug-session: Ephemeral interactive sessions for iterative data flow development.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.exposure-control: Query and configure preview feature rollout state for a factory.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integration-runtime: Enables or disables interactive query on an Azure SSIS integration runtime.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.integration-runtime-object-metadata: SSIS catalog metadata (packages, projects) stored in the IR.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Manages approval or rejection of inbound private endpoint connections.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Lists private link subresource targets supported by the factory.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.private-endpoint-connection: Alias for private endpoint connection management.

services.description: Manage Azure Synapse Analytics workspaces, SQL pools, and administrators
services.detailedDescription: Covers Synapse workspace lifecycle management, Azure AD administrator configuration, SQL pool management, and managed identity SQL control settings. Requires Contributor or Synapse-specific roles.

resourceDescriptions.workspaceaadadmin: Azure AD administrator for Synapse workspaces
resourceDescriptions.workspacesqlaadadmin: Azure AD administrator for Synapse SQL
resourceDescriptions.workspacemanagedidentitysqlcontrolsettin: Managed identity SQL control settings for workspaces

resourceDetailedDescriptions.workspace: Top-level Synapse workspace providing unified analytics across data lake, Spark, and SQL. Use create-or-update to provision with storage account and managed VNet settings.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workspaceaadadmin: Configures an Azure AD user or group as the workspace-level administrator.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workspacesqlaadadmin: Configures an Azure AD user or group as the SQL-level administrator.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workspacemanagedidentitysqlcontrolsettin: Controls whether the workspace managed identity is granted SQL access.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.restorabledroppedsqlpool: Lists and retrieves recently dropped dedicated SQL pools that can be restored.

services.description: Manage Azure Cognitive Services and Azure AI accounts, deployments, and safety policies
services.detailedDescription: Covers Azure Cognitive Services and Azure AI Foundry resource management: accounts, deployments, model management, Responsible AI content filters, commitment plans, RAI policies, and network security. Requires Cognitive Services Contributor or Owner role.

resourceDescriptions.account: Cognitive Services and Azure AI accounts
resourceDescriptions.deployment: Model deployments within a Cognitive Services account
resourceDescriptions.commitmentplan: Commitment plans for reserved capacity pricing
resourceDescriptions.raiblocklist: Custom content block lists for RAI filtering
resourceDescriptions.raiblocklistitem: Individual items within RAI block lists
resourceDescriptions.raitoollabel: Tool usage labels for RAI telemetry
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Private endpoint connections for Cognitive Services accounts
resourceDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Available private link resources
resourceDescriptions.project: Azure AI Foundry projects within an account
resourceDescriptions.projectconnection: Connections within an AI Foundry project
resourceDescriptions.agentapplication: Agent applications within an AI Foundry project
resourceDescriptions.workspacefeature: Features available within AI Foundry workspaces
resourceDescriptions.quotatier: Quota tier management for Cognitive Services

resourceDetailedDescriptions.account: The foundational resource for all Cognitive Services APIs. Use create to provision an account with a specific kind (OpenAI, Speech, Vision, etc.) and SKU.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.deployment: Model deployment within an account, specifying model, version, SKU capacity, and scale settings.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.model: Lists fine-tuned and base models available for deployment in the account's region.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.commitmentplan: Prepaid capacity plans that provide discounted pricing for high-volume usage.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.raipolicy: Content filtering configuration attached to deployments, controlling what content is allowed or blocked.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.raiblocklist: User-defined word/phrase block lists used to extend built-in content filtering.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.raiblocklistitem: Individual blocked terms or patterns within a custom block list.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.raicontentfilter: Microsoft-managed content filters (hate, violence, self-harm, etc.) viewable for reference.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.raitopic: Custom topic definitions that trigger content filtering when matched.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.raitoollabel: Labels applied to tool calls for telemetry and audit purposes.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.deletedaccount: Accounts in the soft-delete retention window; use purge to permanently remove.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Approve or reject inbound private endpoint connections.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Lists available private link subresources for the account.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.encryptionscope: Customer-managed key (CMK) encryption scope for data-at-rest encryption.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.defenderforaisetting: Configures Microsoft Defender for AI threat protection on the account.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.project: Logical workspace within an Azure AI Foundry hub account for organizing experiments and resources.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.projectconnection: Named connections within a project to external services (storage, compute, etc.).
resourceDetailedDescriptions.accountconnection: Named connections at the account hub level shared across projects.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.agentapplication: Deployable AI agent applications built on top of AI Foundry.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.agentdeployment: Provisioned instances of agent applications with associated infrastructure.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workspacefeature: Lists feature flags and capabilities enabled for the AI Foundry workspace.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.quotatier: Manages per-subscription quota tiers for rate limiting and capacity.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.resourcesku: Lists available pricing tiers and capabilities for Cognitive Services kinds.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.commitmenttier: Lists available commitment tier options for prepaid pricing.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.usage: Current quota consumption and limits for the account or subscription.

services.description: Manage Azure Machine Learning workspaces, compute, jobs, models, and deployments
services.detailedDescription: Covers the full Azure Machine Learning resource lifecycle: workspaces, compute targets, training jobs, model and dataset registries, online/batch endpoints and deployments, feature stores, schedules, and managed networks. Requires Contributor or ML-specific roles.

resourceDescriptions.compute: Compute targets attached to a workspace (AmlCompute, AKS, compute instances)
resourceDescriptions.datastore: Data connections (Azure Storage, ADLS, SQL, etc.)
resourceDescriptions.workspaceconnection: External service connections for the workspace
resourceDescriptions.featuresetcontainer: Feature set definitions for feature stores
resourceDescriptions.feature: Individual features within a feature set

resourceDetailedDescriptions.workspace: Central hub for all ML activities: experiments, compute, data, models, and deployments. Use create-or-update to provision with storage, key vault, and container registry.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.compute: Managed compute resources — AmlCompute clusters for training, compute instances for development, or attached AKS for inference.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.job: Executes ML workloads: command jobs (scripts), sweep jobs (hyperparameter tuning), pipeline jobs (multi-step), and AutoML jobs.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.onlineendpoint: HTTP endpoint for real-time scoring. Use get-token to obtain authentication tokens for client calls.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.onlinedeployment: Deployment under an online endpoint specifying model, environment, compute SKU, and traffic split.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.batchendpoint: Asynchronous endpoint for large-scale batch inference using compute clusters.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.batchdeployment: Deployment under a batch endpoint specifying model, environment, and compute target.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.modelcontainer: Name-scoped container grouping all versions of a registered model.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.modelversion: A specific versioned model artifact with path, flavors, and properties.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.environmentcontainer: Name-scoped container grouping all versions of a registered environment.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.environmentversion: A specific versioned environment with Docker image or conda specification.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.datacontainer: Name-scoped container grouping all versions of a registered dataset.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.dataversion: A specific versioned data asset pointing to storage path(s).
resourceDetailedDescriptions.codeversion: A specific versioned code snapshot uploaded for training.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.componentcontainer: Name-scoped container for reusable pipeline components.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.componentversion: A versioned pipeline component definition (inputs, outputs, command).
resourceDetailedDescriptions.datastore: Named connection to a storage account, ADLS Gen2, or SQL database used by jobs and datasets.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.schedule: Triggers a job on a recurring CRON or recurrence schedule.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workspaceconnection: External service credential (storage SAS, API key, service principal) stored in the workspace.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.registry: Cross-workspace asset registry for sharing models, environments, and components across teams.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.serverlessendpoint: Token-based endpoint for serverless inference billed per request, without managing compute.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.featuresetcontainer: Named feature set in the feature store.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.featuresetversion: Versioned feature set with transformation logic and materialization settings.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.featurestoreentitycontainer: Entity definition used as a join key in feature queries.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.feature: An individual feature column with metadata within a feature set.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.marketplacesubscription: Active subscription to a third-party model from Azure Marketplace.

services.description: Manage Azure AI Search services, admin keys, query keys, and private endpoints
services.detailedDescription: Covers Azure AI Search (formerly Azure Cognitive Search) service lifecycle management: creating and scaling search services, managing admin and query API keys, configuring private endpoints, and shared private link resources for indexer data access.

resourceDescriptions.adminkey: Admin API keys for full access to the search service
resourceDescriptions.querykey: Query-only API keys for read-only search access
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Private endpoint connections for the search service
resourceDescriptions.sharedprivatelinkresource: Shared private link resources for indexer outbound access
resourceDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Available private link resources

resourceDetailedDescriptions.service: The top-level Azure AI Search resource. Use create-or-update to provision with SKU (free, basic, standard), replica/partition counts, and network rules.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.adminkey: Admin API keys grant full read/write access to all service operations. Use regenerate to rotate primary or secondary keys.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.querykey: Query API keys are read-only tokens for client-side search. Create multiple keys for different applications.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Manages approval of inbound private endpoint connections to secure the service endpoint.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.sharedprivatelinkresource: Allows indexers to securely access private resources (storage, Cosmos DB, SQL) using service-managed private links.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Lists the private link subresources supported by this search service.

services.description: Manage Azure Databricks workspaces and private endpoints
services.detailedDescription: Covers Azure Databricks workspace lifecycle management and private endpoint connectivity. For Databricks-native resources (clusters, jobs, notebooks), use the Databricks REST API directly.

resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Private endpoint connections for Databricks workspaces
resourceDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Available private link resources

resourceDetailedDescriptions.workspace: The top-level Databricks workspace resource. Use create-or-update to provision with managed resource group, VNet injection, and SKU (standard, premium, trial).
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Manage private endpoint connections for secure workspace access without public internet exposure.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Lists the private link subresources supported by this Databricks workspace.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.outboundnetworkdependenciesendpoint: Lists external FQDN endpoints that the workspace requires for outbound connectivity.

services.description: Manage Azure HDInsight Hadoop, Spark, and Kafka clusters
services.detailedDescription: Covers HDInsight cluster lifecycle: create, resize, rotate encryption keys, manage gateway settings, and retrieve async operation status. For Ambari, YARN, or HDFS management, use the cluster gateway APIs directly.

resourceDescriptions.cluster: HDInsight cluster instances (Hadoop, Spark, Kafka, HBase, Interactive Query)

resourceDetailedDescriptions.cluster: Managed Hadoop-ecosystem cluster. Use create with a cluster definition specifying the cluster kind, worker node SKU and count, storage account, and credentials.

services.description: Manage Azure Stream Analytics streaming jobs
services.detailedDescription: Covers Azure Stream Analytics job lifecycle: creating, configuring, starting, stopping, and scaling streaming jobs that process real-time data from Event Hubs, IoT Hub, or Blob Storage.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.streamingjob: A real-time analytics job that queries incoming data streams using SQL-like queries. Use create-or-replace to define inputs, outputs, queries, and streaming units.

services.description: Manage Azure Data Lake Analytics accounts, compute policies, and linked data stores
services.detailedDescription: Covers Data Lake Analytics account lifecycle, compute policy management, linked Data Lake Store and Azure Storage accounts, and firewall rules. For submitting U-SQL jobs, use the Data Lake Analytics catalog and job APIs.

resourceDescriptions.computepolicy: Compute policies limiting resources per job or user
resourceDescriptions.firewallrule: Firewall rules for network access control

resourceDetailedDescriptions.account: Top-level Data Lake Analytics resource. Use create to provision with default Data Lake Store, degree of parallelism, and pricing tier.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.computepolicy: Limits the maximum AU (Analytics Units) per job or per queued jobs for a specific user or group.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.datalakestoreaccount: Links an Azure Data Lake Store account as a data source for U-SQL jobs.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.storageaccount: Links an Azure Storage account for reading input files or writing output.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.firewallrule: IP range rules controlling which clients can connect to the account.

services.description: Manage Azure Data Lake Storage Gen1 accounts, firewall rules, and trusted ID providers
services.detailedDescription: Covers Data Lake Storage Gen1 account lifecycle, firewall rule configuration, trusted identity provider (ADFS) management, and virtual network rules. For file system operations (upload, download, list), use the Data Lake Store filesystem API.

resourceDescriptions.account: Data Lake Storage Gen1 account instances
resourceDescriptions.firewallrule: Firewall rules for network access control
resourceDescriptions.trustedidprovider: Trusted identity providers (ADFS) for claims-based authentication
resourceDescriptions.virtualnetworkrule: Virtual network rules for VNET-based access control

resourceDetailedDescriptions.account: Top-level Data Lake Storage Gen1 resource. Use create to provision with encryption settings, firewall state, and default access policies.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.firewallrule: IP range rules controlling which clients can access the account endpoint.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.trustedidprovider: Configures an ADFS endpoint as a trusted claims issuer for custom authentication flows.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualnetworkrule: Restricts account access to traffic originating from specific Azure virtual network subnets.

services.description: Manage Azure IoT Hub instances, certificates, consumer groups, and routing
services.detailedDescription: Covers IoT Hub lifecycle management, X.509 certificate management, Event Hub consumer groups, device import/export jobs, routing configuration tests, quota metrics, and endpoint health monitoring. Requires IoT Hub Contributor or Owner role.

resourceDescriptions.iot-hub: IoT Hub failover and management operations
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Private endpoint connections
resourceDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Available private link resources

resourceDetailedDescriptions.resource: The main IoT Hub resource. Use create-or-update to provision with SKU (F1, S1, S2, S3), capacity units, and feature settings.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.certificate: X.509 CA certificates for device enrollment. Use generate-verification-code and verify to complete proof-of-possession.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.iot-hub: Supports manual failover to the paired region for disaster recovery testing.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Approve or reject private endpoint connections to secure IoT Hub access.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Lists the private link subresources available for this IoT Hub.

services.description: Manage Azure IoT Hub Device Provisioning Service instances and certificates
services.detailedDescription: Covers IoT Hub DPS lifecycle management, X.509 certificate management with proof-of-possession, shared access key management, and private endpoint connectivity. Requires IoT Hub Contributor or Owner role.

resourceDescriptions.dps-certificate: X.509 CA certificates for device provisioning

resourceDetailedDescriptions.iot-dps-resource: The top-level DPS resource. Use create-or-update to provision with SKU (S1), capacity units, and linked IoT Hubs.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.dps-certificate: X.509 CA certificates used to auto-enroll devices. Use generate-verification-code and verify to complete proof-of-possession.

services.description: Manage Azure IoT Central application instances
services.detailedDescription: Covers IoT Central SaaS application lifecycle management including subdomain and name availability checks and application template listing. For device management and data export within an application, use the IoT Central REST API.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.app: An IoT Central application with its own subdomain. Use create-or-update to provision from a template with display name, SKU (ST0, ST1, ST2), and subdomain.

services.description: Manage Azure Digital Twins instances, endpoints, and time series database connections
services.detailedDescription: Covers Digital Twins instance lifecycle management, event routing endpoint configuration (Event Hub, Service Bus, Event Grid), time series database (Azure Data Explorer) connections, and private endpoint connectivity.

resourceDescriptions.endpoint: Event routing endpoints for Digital Twins
resourceDescriptions.timeseriesdatabaseconnection: Time series database connections (Azure Data Explorer)
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Private endpoint connections

resourceDetailedDescriptions.digitaltwin: The top-level Digital Twins resource. Use create-or-update to provision in a region with identity settings.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.endpoint: Routes Digital Twins events to external services — Event Hub, Service Bus topic, or Event Grid Topic.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.timeseriesdatabaseconnection: Connects a Digital Twins instance to an Azure Data Explorer cluster for time series data storage and query.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Manage private endpoint connections for secure Digital Twins API access.

services.description: Manage Azure Device Update for IoT Hub accounts and instances
services.detailedDescription: Covers Device Update account and instance lifecycle management for over-the-air (OTA) firmware updates to IoT devices. Requires Device Update Administrator role.

resourceDescriptions.instance: Device Update instances within an account

resourceDetailedDescriptions.account: Top-level Device Update resource. Use create to provision an account in a region; accounts can contain multiple instances.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.instance: A deployment environment within a Device Update account, linked to specific IoT Hubs for OTA update targeting.

services.description: Manage Azure Device Registry assets, asset endpoint profiles, and schema registries
services.detailedDescription: Covers the Azure Device Registry resource hierarchy for industrial IoT: assets (OPC UA, MQTT devices), asset endpoint profiles (connectivity), schema registries for data modeling, and namespace-scoped resources for device and discovered asset management.

resourceDescriptions.asset: IoT assets representing physical devices or OPC UA nodes
resourceDescriptions.assetendpointprofile: Connectivity profiles for asset endpoint communication
resourceDescriptions.schemaregistry: Schema registries for data model definitions
resourceDescriptions.schema: Data schemas within a schema registry
resourceDescriptions.namespace: Device Registry namespaces for organizing assets and devices

resourceDetailedDescriptions.asset: Represents an OPC UA server, MQTT topic, or other industrial device. Contains data points, events, and connectivity settings.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.assetendpointprofile: Defines the transport protocol and authentication for connecting to an asset endpoint (OPC UA, MQTT, etc.).
resourceDetailedDescriptions.schemaregistry: A container for data schemas used to define the structure of telemetry and events from assets.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.schema: A named schema within a registry, grouping multiple versions.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.schemaversion: An immutable version of a schema with the actual schema content (JSON Schema, Delta schema, etc.).
resourceDetailedDescriptions.namespace: An organizational scope for grouping assets and devices within a resource group. Use migrate to upgrade namespace version.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.namespaceasset: An asset resource scoped within a specific namespace.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.namespacedevice: A device resource scoped within a specific namespace.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.billingcontainer: Tracks resource usage and billing information for Device Registry.

services.description: Manage Azure File Sync services, sync groups, server endpoints, and registered servers
services.detailedDescription: Covers Azure File Sync service lifecycle, sync group configuration, cloud endpoint management, server endpoint provisioning on Windows servers, registered server management, and workflow monitoring.

resourceDescriptions.syncgroup: Sync groups defining the sync topology
resourceDescriptions.cloudendpoint: Cloud endpoints (Azure file shares) within a sync group
resourceDescriptions.serverendpoint: Server endpoints (Windows Server paths) within a sync group
resourceDescriptions.registeredserver: Windows servers registered with a File Sync service
resourceDescriptions.workflow: Async workflow operations for the File Sync service
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Private endpoint connections

resourceDetailedDescriptions.service: The top-level File Sync resource. Use create to provision in a region with incoming traffic policy settings.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.syncgroup: Defines a group of endpoints that sync to each other. Contains one cloud endpoint and one or more server endpoints.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.cloudendpoint: Represents an Azure file share in the sync group. Supports backup integration via pre/post-backup and change detection.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.serverendpoint: Represents a path on a registered Windows Server that participates in sync. Supports tiering policy and recall.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.registeredserver: A Windows Server registered with the File Sync service for management and sync. Use trigger-rollover to initiate certificate rotation.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workflow: Tracks long-running operations on the service. Use abort to cancel a running workflow.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Manages private endpoint connections for secure service access.

services.description: Manage Azure Storage Mover resources for cloud data migration
services.detailedDescription: Covers Storage Mover service lifecycle, migration projects, source and target endpoints, job definitions for migration tasks, and agent management for on-premises data transfer.

resourceDescriptions.project: Migration projects grouping related job definitions
resourceDescriptions.endpoint: Source and target endpoints (storage accounts, NFS shares, SMB shares)
resourceDescriptions.jobdefinition: Job definitions specifying source, target, and transfer settings
resourceDescriptions.connection: Connections between agents and Storage Mover services

resourceDetailedDescriptions.storagemover: The top-level Storage Mover resource. Use create-or-update to provision a migration service in a region.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.project: Groups related job definitions for organizational purposes. Helps track migration progress across related workloads.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.endpoint: Represents a storage location: Azure Blob container, Azure Files share, NFS mount, or SMB share.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.agent: A software agent installed on-premises that performs the actual data transfer operations.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.jobdefinition: Specifies a migration task: source endpoint, target endpoint, copy mode, and schedule. Use start-job / stop-job to control execution.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.jobrun: An immutable record of a single execution of a job definition with status, error details, and statistics.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.connection: Manages connectivity between agents and the Storage Mover service.

services.description: Manage Azure HPC Cache and Azure Managed Lustre file systems
services.detailedDescription: Covers HPC Cache lifecycle management (create, flush, start/stop, firmware upgrades, priming jobs), storage target configuration, and Azure Managed Lustre (AML) filesystem management with import/export jobs.

resourceDescriptions.storagetarget: Storage targets connected to an HPC Cache
resourceDescriptions.amlfilesystem: Azure Managed Lustre file system instances
resourceDescriptions.importjob: Import jobs for AML file systems
resourceDescriptions.autoexportjob: Auto-export jobs for AML file systems
resourceDescriptions.autoimportjob: Auto-import jobs for AML file systems
resourceDescriptions.expansionjob: Expansion jobs for growing AML file system capacity

resourceDetailedDescriptions.cache: The main HPC Cache resource providing low-latency NFS access to back-end storage. Use flush to sync data to storage targets.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.storagetarget: Connects HPC Cache to Azure Blob or NFS storage. Use invalidate to force cache eviction for a target.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.amlfilesystem: A fully managed Lustre parallel file system. Use archive/cancel-archive to manage HSM operations.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.importjob: Imports data from Azure Blob Storage into an AML filesystem namespace.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.autoexportjob: Automatically exports new or modified files from AML filesystem to Azure Blob Storage.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.autoimportjob: Automatically imports new or modified blobs from Azure Blob Storage into AML filesystem.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.expansionjob: Expands the storage capacity of an AML filesystem.

services.description: Manage Azure Elastic SAN instances, volume groups, volumes, and snapshots
services.detailedDescription: Covers Elastic SAN (Storage Area Network) lifecycle management: SAN provisioning, volume group creation with iSCSI or NFS protocols, volume management, volume snapshots, and private endpoint connectivity.

resourceDescriptions.volumegroup: Volume groups within an Elastic SAN
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Private endpoint connections

resourceDetailedDescriptions.elasticsan: The top-level Elastic SAN resource. Use create to provision with base and extended size in TiB and redundancy settings.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.volumegroup: A logical grouping of volumes with shared protocol (iSCSI or NFS), encryption, and network rules.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.volume: A block storage volume. Use pre-backup and pre-restore to coordinate with backup solutions.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.volumesnapshot: A crash-consistent point-in-time copy of a volume for backup or cloning.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Manages private endpoint connections for secure volume access.

services.description: Manage Azure NetApp Files accounts, capacity pools, volumes, snapshots, and backup vaults
services.detailedDescription: Covers the full Azure NetApp Files hierarchy: NetApp accounts, capacity pools, volumes (with cross-region replication, cloning, ransomware protection, and quota management), snapshots, snapshot policies, backup vaults, and subvolumes.

resourceDescriptions.pool: Capacity pools within a NetApp account
resourceDescriptions.volume: NFS/SMB volumes within a capacity pool
resourceDescriptions.snapshot: Volume snapshots for backup and recovery
resourceDescriptions.backupvault: Backup vaults for long-term volume backup storage
resourceDescriptions.backup: Volume backups stored in a backup vault
resourceDescriptions.subvolume: Subvolumes within a volume for multi-tenant scenarios
resourceDescriptions.volumegroup: Volume groups for SAP HANA and Oracle deployments
resourceDescriptions.volumequotarule: User and group quota rules for volumes

resourceDetailedDescriptions.account: Top-level NetApp resource in a region. Use change-key-vault to configure CMK encryption.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.pool: Defines a pool of capacity with a service level (Ultra, Premium, Standard). Volumes are created within pools.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.volume: An NFS or SMB volume. Supports cross-region replication, cloning from snapshots, dynamic pool change, and ransomware protection.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.snapshot: A point-in-time snapshot of a volume. Use restore-files to recover individual files.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.snapshotpolicy: Automates snapshot creation on hourly, daily, weekly, or monthly schedules.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.backupvault: Container for backup data. Backups from multiple volumes can be stored in a single vault.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.backup: A backup of a volume stored in a vault. Supports file-level restore.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.backuppolicy: Automates backup creation on daily, weekly, or monthly schedules.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.subvolume: A namespace path within a volume for multi-tenant file access patterns.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.volumegroup: Deploys multiple volumes with pre-configured settings for SAP HANA or Oracle workloads.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.volumequotarule: Applies disk quota limits to specific users or groups within a volume.

services.description: Manage Azure Data Box job orders for offline data transfer
services.detailedDescription: Covers Data Box job lifecycle management: creating orders for physical device-based bulk data transfer, tracking shipment status, managing device credentials, and canceling orders.

resourceDescriptions.job: Data Box job orders for physical device data transfer

resourceDetailedDescriptions.job: A Data Box order specifying the device type (Data Box, Data Box Disk, Data Box Heavy), data transfer direction, storage destination, and shipping contact. Use list-credentials to retrieve device unlock credentials.

services.description: Manage Azure Stack Edge and Data Box Edge devices, shares, and compute roles
services.detailedDescription: Covers Azure Stack Edge device lifecycle management, shares, users, storage accounts, compute roles, triggers, bandwidth schedules, diagnostic settings, and support package generation.

resourceDescriptions.device: Azure Stack Edge / Data Box Edge device instances
resourceDescriptions.storageaccount: Storage accounts on the device for local blob storage
resourceDescriptions.role: Compute roles (IoT Edge, Kubernetes) on the device
resourceDescriptions.trigger: File event or periodic triggers for IoT workloads
resourceDescriptions.container: Blob containers in device storage accounts
resourceDescriptions.job: Async job status tracking for device operations
resourceDescriptions.diagnosticsetting: Remote diagnostics and proactive log collection settings
resourceDescriptions.node: Physical nodes in a cluster device

resourceDetailedDescriptions.device: The main Azure Stack Edge resource. Use download-updates/install-updates for firmware management, generate-certificate for device authentication.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.share: Network-accessible file share on the device. Can be linked to Azure Storage for cloud tiering.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.user: Local device user with credentials for share access.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.storageaccount: Local blob storage endpoint on the device, optionally synced to Azure Storage.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.storageaccountcredential: Credentials (SAS or account key) for accessing external Azure Storage accounts.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.role: IoT Edge, Kubernetes, or other compute roles enabling workload execution on the device.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.addon: Extends a Kubernetes role with Arc, monitoring, or other capabilities.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.trigger: Fires on file events or on a recurring schedule to invoke IoT Edge modules.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.bandwidthschedule: Throttles network bandwidth usage during specified time windows.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.order: Tracks device order status, shipment, and delivery. Use list-dc-access-code for datacenter operations.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.alert: Read-only view of device health alerts.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.container: A blob container within a device storage account.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.job: Read-only job tracking for long-running device operations.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.diagnosticsetting: Controls remote support access and proactive log collection settings.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.node: Read-only view of physical nodes in a cluster-configured device.

services.description: Manage Azure Storage Actions tasks for automated storage operations
services.detailedDescription: Covers Storage Actions task lifecycle management for automating operations on Azure Blob Storage (e.g., tiering, tagging, deletion based on conditions). Task assignments and execution reports are read-only.

resourceDetailedDescriptions.storagetask: A task definition containing conditions and operations to apply to blobs. Use preview-actions to test the task against sample data before deployment.

services.description: Manage Azure Recovery Services vaults for backup and site recovery.
services.detailedDescription: Covers vault lifecycle (create/update/delete), extended info, vault certificates, registered identities, replication usages, and private link resources. Used as the control-plane for both Azure Backup and Azure Site Recovery. Requires Contributor or Backup Contributor role on the target subscription or resource group.

resourceDescriptions.vaultextendedinfo: Extended vault information and encryption settings
resourceDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Private link resources for vault private endpoints
resourceDescriptions.recoveryservice: Recovery Services capabilities and name availability checks

resourceDetailedDescriptions.vault: Create, update, delete, and retrieve Recovery Services vaults. Vaults are the top-level container for backup and site-recovery data.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.deletedvault: List and retrieve soft-deleted vaults, or trigger undelete to restore a vault from soft-delete state.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.vaultextendedinfo: Manage vault extended information including resource guard protection and key vault encryption settings.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.vaultcertificate: Upload certificates used by on-premises agents and DPM servers to authenticate with the Recovery Services vault.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privatelinkresource: Enumerate private link resources available on a vault for configuring private endpoint connectivity.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.registeredidentity: Delete registered identities (backup containers) that have been registered with the vault.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.replicationusage: Retrieve Azure Site Recovery replication usage metrics for monitoring replication health.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.usage: Query usage summaries and quota consumption for a Recovery Services vault.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.recoveryservice: Check Recovery Services feature capabilities and validate vault name availability before creation.

services.description: Manage Azure Backup for VMs, files, SQL, SAP HANA, and other workloads.
services.detailedDescription: Covers backup policy and instance management, protection containers, protected items, restore operations, job monitoring, and resource vault configuration. Supports IaaS VMs, Azure Files, SQL Server, SAP HANA, and DPM/MABS workloads. Requires Backup Contributor or Backup Operator role.

resourceDescriptions.protectionpolicy: Backup policies defining schedule, retention, and tier settings
resourceDescriptions.protecteditem: Protected backup items (VMs, files, databases) within a vault
resourceDescriptions.protectioncontainer: Backup containers that group protected items by workload type
resourceDescriptions.recoverypoint: Available recovery points for restore operations
resourceDescriptions.restore: Trigger restore operations from recovery points
resourceDescriptions.policy: Backup policies listing at the vault level
resourceDescriptions.protectableitem: Discoverable items eligible for backup protection
resourceDescriptions.workloaditem: SQL/SAP workload items within a registered container
resourceDescriptions.engine: Backup engines (DPM/MABS servers) registered with the vault
resourceDescriptions.resourcevaultconfig: Vault-level security and cross-region restore configuration
resourceDescriptions.resourceencryptionconfig: Customer-managed key encryption configuration for backup data
resourceDescriptions.securitypin: Security PIN for critical backup operations
resourceDescriptions.usagesummary: Vault backup usage and storage consumption summaries
resourceDescriptions.recoverypoint-recommended-for-move: Recovery points recommended for tiering moves
resourceDescriptions.protectionintent: Pre-registration protection intent for Azure workloads
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Private endpoint connections to the backup vault
resourceDescriptions.resourceguardproxy: Resource Guard proxy for MUA (multi-user authorization) protection
resourceDescriptions.backupstatus: Backup protection status for a VM or workload
resourceDescriptions.itemlevelrecoveryconnection: Item-level recovery (ILR) connections for file restore

resourceDetailedDescriptions.protectionpolicy: Create, update, and delete backup policies. Policies control backup schedules, retention durations, and tiering rules for IaaS VMs, Files, SQL, and SAP workloads.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.protecteditem: Manage protected backup items: enable protection, update policy assignment, trigger ad-hoc backup, and remove protection. Supports VMs, Azure Files, SQL databases, and SAP HANA.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.protectioncontainer: Register, unregister, inquire, and refresh backup containers. Used with DPM servers, MABS, and Azure workload containers.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.recoverypoint: List and retrieve available recovery points for a protected item. Includes instant, standard, and archived recovery points.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.restore: Trigger a restore operation from a recovery point to an original or alternate location.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.job: List, monitor, and export backup jobs. Use job export for large result sets that exceed API page limits.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.policy: List all backup policies in a vault. Use protectionpolicy for create/update/delete operations.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.protectableitem: Enumerate workload-specific items (SQL databases, SAP HANA databases) that can be protected.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.protectablecontainer: Enumerate containers (storage accounts, SQL instances) that can be registered with a vault.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workloaditem: List SQL Server databases and SAP HANA instances within a registered backup container.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.engine: List and get DPM or MABS backup engines registered with the vault.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.resourcevaultconfig: Read or update vault security settings including soft-delete, cross-region restore, and immutability.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.resourceencryptionconfig: Get or update the customer-managed key encryption configuration for backup vault storage.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.securitypin: Retrieve a security PIN required to perform critical operations like disabling soft-delete.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.usagesummary: List storage usage summaries broken down by data source type and replication type.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.recoverypoint-recommended-for-move: List recovery points that Azure recommends moving to a cheaper storage tier.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.protectionintent: Manage pre-registration protection intent for Azure workloads before full protection is configured.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Approve, reject, or delete private endpoint connections to the Recovery Services vault.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.resourceguardproxy: Manage Resource Guard proxy objects that enforce multi-user authorization for destructive backup operations.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.backupstatus: Check whether a VM or workload is currently protected by Azure Backup.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.itemlevelrecoveryconnection: Provision or revoke item-level recovery connections for browsing and restoring individual files from VM backups.

services.description: Manage Azure Backup vaults and backup instances for modern workloads.
services.detailedDescription: Azure Backup (Data Protection) for disks, blobs, PostgreSQL, AKS, and other Azure-native workloads. Covers vault and backup policy lifecycle, backup instance operations (backup, restore, stop, resume), deleted backup management, and Resource Guard integration for multi-user authorization.

resourceDescriptions.backupvault: Backup vault lifecycle (create/update/delete) and name availability checks
resourceDescriptions.backuppolicy: Backup policies defining schedules and retention for disk, blob, and database workloads
resourceDescriptions.backupinstance: Backup instances representing protected data sources with operations for backup, restore, and protection management
resourceDescriptions.recoverypoint: Recovery points available for restore from a backup instance
resourceDescriptions.job: Backup job monitoring for vault operations
resourceDescriptions.deletedbackupinstance: Soft-deleted backup instances with undelete capability
resourceDescriptions.resourceguard: Resource Guard resources for multi-user authorization enforcement
resourceDescriptions.dppresourceguardproxy: Resource Guard proxies for MUA-protected vault operations
resourceDescriptions.restorabletimerange: Continuous backup restorable time ranges for blob and PostgreSQL
resourceDescriptions.dataprotection: Feature support checks for backup capabilities

resourceDetailedDescriptions.backupvault: Create, update, and delete Backup vaults. Vaults are the top-level container for disk backup, blob backup, and PostgreSQL backup instances.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.backuppolicy: Define backup schedules, retention rules, and life-cycle tiers. Policies are assigned to backup instances at time of protection.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.backupinstance: Manage backup instances: enable protection, trigger ad-hoc backup, restore, stop, resume, suspend, and synchronize. Supports disks, blobs, PostgreSQL, and AKS.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.recoverypoint: List and retrieve recovery points for a backup instance. Used to select a restore point before triggering a restore.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.job: Monitor and query backup jobs for on-demand backups, restores, and scheduled backup runs.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.deletedbackupinstance: List and retrieve soft-deleted backup instances, or undelete to restore protection.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.deletedbackupvault: List soft-deleted backup vaults by location or retrieve a specific deleted vault.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.resourceguard: Manage Resource Guard resources that enforce multi-user authorization for destructive operations on vaults and policies.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.dppresourceguardproxy: Create and manage Resource Guard proxy objects that connect a Backup vault to a Resource Guard for MUA protection.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.restorabletimerange: Find the restorable time ranges for continuous backup sources such as blobs and PostgreSQL databases.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.dataprotection: Check feature support for specific backup capabilities before enabling protection.

services.description: Manage Azure Site Recovery (ASR) vaults, fabrics, and replicated items.
services.detailedDescription: Azure Site Recovery for disaster recovery of on-premises and Azure VMs. Covers vault and fabric lifecycle, fabric agents (mobility service), protected items with failover, replication policies, recovery points, and private endpoint connectivity. Requires Site Recovery Contributor or Operator role.

resourceDescriptions.fabric: ASR fabrics representing on-premises sites or Azure regions
resourceDescriptions.fabricagent: Fabric agents (mobility service/replication appliance) registered with a fabric
resourceDescriptions.policy: Replication policies defining RPO, recovery point history, and app-consistent frequency
resourceDescriptions.protecteditem: Protected items (replicated VMs) with failover and failback operations
resourceDescriptions.recoverypoint: Recovery points for a protected item used for failover
resourceDescriptions.job: ASR job tracking for long-running replication operations
resourceDescriptions.replicationextension: Replication extensions connecting source and target fabrics
resourceDescriptions.event: ASR events and alerts for replication health monitoring
resourceDescriptions.emailconfiguration: Email notification configuration for ASR alerts
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Private endpoint connections to the ASR vault

resourceDetailedDescriptions.vault: Create, update, and delete ASR replication vaults. Vaults are the top-level resource for disaster recovery configuration.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.fabric: Manage fabrics representing on-premises VMware/Hyper-V sites or Azure regions. Each fabric contains fabric agents and protected items.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.fabricagent: Register and manage fabric agents (mobility service appliances) associated with a fabric.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.policy: Create and delete replication policies that control RPO targets, recovery point history, and crash-consistent/app-consistent snapshot frequency.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.protecteditem: Enable and manage VM replication: create protection, update replication settings, trigger planned failover, and delete protection.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.recoverypoint: List and retrieve recovery points for a protected VM for use in failover operations.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.job: Monitor and query ASR jobs for replication tasks such as initial replication, planned failover, and test failover.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.replicationextension: Manage replication extensions that link source fabrics to target fabrics for cross-site replication.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.event: List and retrieve ASR events for monitoring replication health, warnings, and informational messages.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.emailconfiguration: Configure email notification recipients and alert thresholds for ASR vault events.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Manage private endpoint connections for private network access to the ASR vault.

services.description: Manage Azure Image Builder image templates and automated VM image builds.
services.detailedDescription: Create and manage image templates that define a source image, customization steps, and a distribution target. Trigger image builds, monitor run outputs, and manage build triggers. Requires Contributor role and a managed identity with permissions to distribute images.

resourceDescriptions.virtualmachineimagetemplate: Image templates defining source, customization steps, and distribution targets
resourceDescriptions.trigger: Triggers that automatically initiate image builds on a schedule or source update

resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualmachineimagetemplate: Create, update, and delete image templates. Run or cancel image builds, and retrieve run outputs including the built image location.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.trigger: Create and manage triggers that automatically start image template builds on a schedule or when a source image is updated.

services.description: Manage Azure Fleet resources for managing multiple VM scale sets as a single unit.
services.detailedDescription: Azure Fleet lets you create and manage a group of Virtual Machine Scale Sets across different VM types and Spot/On-Demand configurations. Useful for large-scale workloads that need to spread across VM SKUs to maximize capacity and cost efficiency.

resourceDescriptions.fleet: Azure Fleet resources grouping multiple VM scale sets

resourceDetailedDescriptions.fleet: Create, update, and delete Azure Fleet resources. List virtual machine scale sets belonging to a fleet, and manage fleet configuration including spot and on-demand VM type preferences.

services.description: Manage Standby Pools for pre-provisioned VMs and container groups to reduce scale-out latency.
services.detailedDescription: Standby Pool maintains a pool of pre-provisioned virtual machines or container groups in standby state so that scale-out operations can be serviced immediately. Covers pool lifecycle, runtime views for monitoring standby capacity, and individual standby VM management.

resourceDescriptions.virtualmachinepool: Standby pools of pre-provisioned virtual machines
resourceDescriptions.virtualmachine: Individual standby virtual machines within a pool
resourceDescriptions.virtualmachinepoolruntimeview: Runtime view showing current standby VM counts and state
resourceDescriptions.containergrouppool: Standby pools of pre-provisioned container groups
resourceDescriptions.containergrouppoolruntimeview: Runtime view showing current standby container group counts and state

resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualmachinepool: Create, update, and delete standby VM pools. Configure the maximum ready capacity and the VM profile used for standby instances.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualmachine: List and retrieve individual standby virtual machines within a standby VM pool.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.virtualmachinepoolruntimeview: View real-time runtime statistics for a standby VM pool including current instance counts by state.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.containergrouppool: Create, update, and delete standby container group pools for ACI workloads that require rapid scale-out.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.containergrouppoolruntimeview: View real-time runtime statistics for a standby container group pool.

services.description: Schedule and submit compute operations (start/stop/deallocate) for Azure VMs.
services.detailedDescription: Azure Compute Schedule lets you submit and execute scheduled compute operations on virtual machines including start, deallocate, hibernate, and delete. Operations can be submitted for future execution or executed immediately. Track operation status and errors by operation ID.

resourceDescriptions.scheduled-action: Scheduled and immediate compute operations (start/stop/deallocate/hibernate) on virtual machines

resourceDetailedDescriptions.scheduled-action: Submit VMs for future scheduled operations or execute operations immediately. Supported operations include start, deallocate, hibernate, and delete. Cancel pending operations or query status and error details by operation ID.

services.description: Manage Azure Virtual Desktop host pools, application groups, and workspaces.
services.detailedDescription: Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD) for deploying and managing virtual desktop infrastructure. Covers host pool lifecycle, application groups and applications, workspaces, session hosts, user sessions, MSIX app attach packages, and scaling plans with personal and pooled schedules. Requires Desktop Virtualization Contributor or Owner role.

resourceDescriptions.hostpool: Host pools grouping session hosts for load balancing and access management
resourceDescriptions.applicationgroup: Application groups publishing remote desktops or applications from a host pool
resourceDescriptions.application: RemoteApp applications published within an application group
resourceDescriptions.workspace: Workspaces aggregating application groups for user access
resourceDescriptions.sessionhost: Virtual machine session hosts registered in a host pool
resourceDescriptions.usersession: Active and disconnected user sessions on session hosts
resourceDescriptions.scalingplan: Scaling plans defining automated scaling schedules for host pools
resourceDescriptions.scalingplanpooledschedule: Scheduled scaling rules for pooled host pools
resourceDescriptions.scalingplanpersonalschedule: Scheduled scaling rules for personal host pools
resourceDescriptions.appattachpackage: MSIX app attach packages for application delivery
resourceDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Private endpoint connections to host pools and workspaces
resourceDescriptions.desktop: Remote desktop resources published within a desktop application group

resourceDetailedDescriptions.hostpool: Create, update, and delete host pools. Retrieve registration tokens for joining session hosts, manage host pool settings including load balancing type, RDP properties, and validation environment.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.applicationgroup: Create and manage application groups of type Desktop or RemoteApp. Control which host pool the group is associated with and assign users via role assignments.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.application: Publish and manage RemoteApp applications within an application group. Configure application display name, file path, command line settings, and icons.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.workspace: Create and manage workspaces that aggregate application groups for user-facing portal access.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.sessionhost: List, update, and delete session hosts in a host pool. View session host health, agent version, and availability for new sessions.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.usersession: List, disconnect, and log off user sessions on session hosts. Send messages to active users.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.scalingplan: Create scaling plans that automatically adjust session host availability based on time-of-day schedules.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.scalingplanpooledschedule: Define scaling schedules for pooled desktop host pools including ramp-up, peak, ramp-down, and off-peak phases.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.scalingplanpersonalschedule: Define scaling schedules for personal host pools controlling when session hosts are deallocated.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.appattachpackage: Import and manage MSIX app attach packages for deploying applications without installing them on session hosts.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.privateendpointconnection: Approve, reject, or delete private endpoint connections to AVD host pools and workspaces.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.desktop: Retrieve and update friendly names and descriptions for desktops published in a desktop application group.

services.description: Manage Azure Arc-enabled servers and hybrid compute machines.
services.detailedDescription: Azure Arc for servers: onboard and manage Windows and Linux machines outside Azure as Arc-enabled servers. Covers machine lifecycle, extensions, run commands, licensing, network profiles, and gateway connectivity. Requires Azure Connected Machine Contributor or Owner role.

resourceDescriptions.machine: Arc-enabled server (hybrid compute machine) lifecycle and patch operations
resourceDescriptions.machineextension: VM extensions installed on Arc-enabled servers
resourceDescriptions.machineruncommand: Run commands executed on Arc-enabled servers
resourceDescriptions.license: Azure Hybrid Benefit licenses assigned to Arc-enabled servers
resourceDescriptions.licenseprofile: License profile settings on an Arc-enabled machine
resourceDescriptions.gateway: Arc gateways for connecting Arc-enabled servers through a gateway proxy
resourceDescriptions.setting: Hybrid compute settings at the subscription scope
resourceDescriptions.agentversion: Available Arc agent versions for Arc-enabled servers
resourceDescriptions.networkprofile: Network profile showing interfaces on an Arc-enabled server

resourceDetailedDescriptions.machine: List, update, and delete Arc-enabled server resources. Trigger on-demand patch assessment and install patches on Arc-connected machines.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.machineextension: Install, update, and remove VM extensions on Arc-enabled servers. Extensions include MMA, AMA, and custom script extensions.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.machineruncommand: Create and manage run commands that execute scripts on Arc-enabled servers for remote management tasks.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.license: Create and assign Windows Server Azure Hybrid Benefit licenses to Arc-enabled servers to reduce licensing costs.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.licenseprofile: Manage the license profile on an Arc-enabled machine including ESU enrollment and product type.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.gateway: Create Arc gateways that allow Arc-enabled servers in restricted networks to connect to Azure through a centralized proxy endpoint.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.setting: View and update subscription-level settings for the Hybrid Compute resource provider.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.agentversion: List available Arc agent versions to understand upgrade paths for Arc-enabled servers.
resourceDetailedDescriptions.networkprofile: Retrieve network interface details for an Arc-enabled server including IP configurations.
